basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: al-Naḥl — “The Bee” — is preeminently the sūra of blessings (niʿam). Even before naming the insect that gives it its title, it draws up an awestruck inventory of graces: the cattle from which come clothing, mount, and nourishment; the water from the sky that revives the dead earth and brings forth crops, olives, date palms, and vines; the night and the day, the sun, the moon, and the stars subjected to His command; the sea with its tender flesh and its ornaments drawn from the waves. Then comes the bee, to whom “your Lord has revealed” to build its dwelling and follow humbly the ways of its Lord: from its belly comes “a drink of varied colors in which there is a healing for people.” The sūra warns: “if you would count the favors of God, you could not number them.” It culminates at the verse of justice — “God enjoins justice, excellence (iḥsān), and giving to kinsfolk” — and is sealed upon the call: “Call to the way of your Lord with wisdom and beautiful exhortation.” Qushayrī reads it as a key of awakening: he who soars like the bird in the void is held aloft by none but God, and the Real has deposited every precious thing within the lowliest — honey in the weakest of winged creatures, as He has deposited His gnosis and His love in the hearts of the believers.

versets.ar: أَتَىٰ أَمْرُ اللَّهِ فَلَا تَسْتَعْجِلُوهُ ۚ سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَىٰ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: atā amru llāhi fa-lā tastaʿjilūhu subḥānahu wa-taʿālā ʿammā yushrikūna
versets.trad: The command of God is coming, so do not seek to hasten it. Glory to Him, and may He be exalted above what they associate with Him.
versets.commentaire: The past tense “has come” speaks of the future: the Hour will surely arrive. Qushayrī recalls that every event, all good and evil, all benefit and harm, the sweet and the bitter, belongs to His command, by His foreordainment and measure. The people of unity (tawḥīd) do not hasten anything by their own choice, for wills and demands have fallen away from them; they remain extinguished beneath the flow of the decrees, with neither preference nor election. When a verdict of destiny appears, they receive it with a smiling face and welcome whatever surges from the Unseen — refusal or gift, denial or opening — under the sign of contentment, praising the Real in all things.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / أ م ر
mots.sens: the command of God has come
mots.def: amr: command, decree, here the Hour; past tense for the certainty of the future.

mots.sens: do not seek to hasten it
mots.def: istaʿjala: to wish to hasten, to press.

versets.ar: يُنَزِّلُ الْمَلَائِكَةَ بِالرُّوحِ مِنْ أَمْرِهِ عَلَىٰ مَن يَشَاءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ أَنْ أَنذِرُوا أَنَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا أَنَا فَاتَّقُونِ
versets.tr: yunazzilu l-malāʾikata bi-r-rūḥi min amrihi ʿalā man yashāʾu min ʿibādihi an andhirū annahu lā ilāha illā anā fa-ttaqūni
versets.trad: He sends down the angels with the Spirit, which proceeds from His command, upon whom He wills among His servants, charging them to give this warning: “In truth, there is no god but Me, so fear Me!”
versets.commentaire: The angels descend upon the prophets with revelation and the message. Qushayrī adds a second descent: the one that comes through intimate knowing and inspiration upon the secrets of the masters of unity — those called the “inspired ones” (muḥaddathūn). This descent upon their hearts is not denied, yet they are not ordered to speak of it nor to carry a message to creatures. The Spirit (rūḥ) denotes revelation and the Qurʾān, and more broadly that which is the cause of life: life of the heart or life of this world.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / م ل ك
mots.def: nazzala: to send down by degrees.
rūḥ: the Spirit, revelation, principle of life.

versets.ar: خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ بِالْحَقِّ ۚ تَعَالَىٰ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: khalaqa s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa bi-l-ḥaqqi taʿālā ʿammā yushrikūna
versets.trad: He created the heavens and the earth in truth. Exalted is He above what they associate with Him!
versets.commentaire: He created them in truth and governs them in truth; He is in the right concerning His creation, for that is His to do. This includes His charging of creatures with obligation, and what follows it: the gathering, the raising, reward and punishment. Qushayrī marks His transcendence: hallowing and honoring Him above having any associate or peer. The truth of creation is itself a sign against all partnership.

versets.ar: خَلَقَ الْإِنسَانَ مِن نُّطْفَةٍ فَإِذَا هُوَ خَصِيمٌ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: khalaqa l-insāna min nuṭfatin fa-idhā huwa khaṣīmun mubīnun
versets.trad: He created man from a drop of fluid, and behold, he becomes an open disputer.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the verse a disclosure of divine power to those endowed with reason: He has made known the perfection of His power, telling that He was able to fashion man — with his marvelous composition and subtle assembly — from a drop whose parts are alike at the moment of origin, yet differentiated when limbs appear and emerge from concealment. Then He instilled in him discernment, intellect, speech, action, and dominion over the animals by way of subjection. That such a creature should turn into an open disputer is the wonder.

versets.ar: وَالْأَنْعَامَ خَلَقَهَا ۗ لَكُمْ فِيهَا دِفْءٌ وَمَنَافِعُ وَمِنْهَا تَأْكُلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-l-anʿāma khalaqahā lakum fīhā difʾun wa-manāfiʿu wa-minhā taʾkulūna
versets.trad: And the cattle He created for you; in them is warmth and uses, and from them you eat.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads it as a reminder of grace: He reminded them of His favor and informed them of the blessings in the animals, and of the manifold benefits they hold for them in every state — bearing burdens, traveling upon them, crossing distances, reaching their ends on their backs, and the benefits of their offspring and their milk. The “warmth” of clothing and the nourishment are signs of a Lord who provides.

versets.ar: وَلَكُمْ فِيهَا جَمَالٌ حِينَ تُرِيحُونَ وَحِينَ تَسْرَحُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lakum fīhā jamālun ḥīna turīḥūna wa-ḥīna tasraḥūna
versets.trad: And there is beauty in them for you when you bring them home at evening and when you drive them out to pasture.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws a contrast: the rich man has beauty through his wealth, the poor man self-sufficiency through his state — and how far apart they are! The wealthy adorn themselves with their cattle when they bring them in and drive them out, while the poor find their sufficiency in their Master at every dawn and dusk. The former have their burdens carried by their beasts; for the latter, the Real carries their burdens from off their hearts.

mots.def: arāḥa: to bring back at dusk.
saraḥa: to send out to graze.

versets.ar: وَتَحْمِلُ أَثْقَالَكُمْ إِلَىٰ بَلَدٍ لَّمْ تَكُونُوا بَالِغِيهِ إِلَّا بِشِقِّ الْأَنفُسِ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكُمْ لَرَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-taḥmilu athqālakum ilā baladin lam takūnū bālighīhi illā bi-shiqqi l-anfusi inna rabbakum la-raʾūfun raḥīmun
versets.trad: They bear your loads to a land you could not reach except with great hardship to yourselves. Indeed your Lord is most kind, compassionate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two kinds of travelers: some are people whose states are the endurance of hardships, joining march to march; others are carried by their Master, far from the toil of contriving, at rest in witnessing the divine ordainment, content with the Real's choice in the hard and the easy. The first bear their loads upon their beasts; the second are borne by their Lord.

mots.racine: ح م ل / ث ق ل
ش ق ق / ن ف س
ر أ ف / ر ح م

versets.ar: وَالْخَيْلَ وَالْبِغَالَ وَالْحَمِيرَ لِتَرْكَبُوهَا وَزِينَةً ۚ وَيَخْلُقُ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-l-khayla wa-l-bighāla wa-l-ḥamīra li-tarkabūhā wa-zīnatan wa-yakhluqu mā lā taʿlamūna
versets.trad: And the horses, the mules, and the donkeys, for you to ride and for adornment. And He creates what you do not know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī takes “what you do not know” into the unseen: just as the believers among the people of Paradise will find in the hereafter what no eye has seen, no ear heard, nor heart of man conceived — so the masters of realities find today what never crossed a mind, what they never read in a book nor learned from a teacher. And how could anyone comprehend what the Real has said He alone knows the detail of?

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ع ل م
mots.sens: He creates what you know not

versets.ar: وَعَلَى اللَّهِ قَصْدُ السَّبِيلِ وَمِنْهَا جَائِرٌ ۚ وَلَوْ شَاءَ لَهَدَاكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ
versets.tr: wa-ʿalā llāhi qaṣdu s-sabīli wa-minhā jāʾirun wa-law shāʾa la-hadākum ajmaʿīna
versets.trad: Upon God rests the showing of the straight way, though some turn from it. Had He willed, He would have guided you all.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī contrasts two parties: one whom He guided to the way and acquainted with the proof, turning from their hearts the promptings of doubt, preserving them from denial and association, raising in their hearts the sun of gnosis and singling them out with the light of clear evidence; and others whom He led astray and blinded to the witnessing of proofs, abasing them in His prior decree without cause — yet had He willed, He would have made them know and guided them.

mots.racine: ق ص د / س ب ل
mots.def: qaṣd: aim, direct course; sabīl: path.

mots.racine: ه د ي / ج م ع
mots.sens: He would have guided you all

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي أَنزَلَ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مَاءً ۖ لَّكُم مِّنْهُ شَرَابٌ وَمِنْهُ شَجَرٌ فِيهِ تُسِيمُونَ
versets.tr: huwa lladhī anzala mina s-samāʾi māʾan lakum minhu sharābun wa-minhu shajarun fīhi tusīmūna
versets.trad: It is He who sends down water from the sky; from it you have drink, and from it grow shrubs among which you pasture your herds.
versets.commentaire: He sent down the rain and made it the watering of vegetation, ran the custom of sustaining life through it, causing trees to grow, fruits to come forth, and rivers to flow. Qushayrī notes the sequence that runs through these verses — “for a people who reflect,” then “for a people who reason,” then “for a people who remember” — and reads in it the order of knowing: first reflection, then knowledge, then remembrance, the abiding of the gaze upon its object.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / س م و / م و ه
mots.sens: sent down water from the sky
mots.def: anzala: to send down; māʾ: water.

versets.ar: يُنبِتُ لَكُم بِهِ الزَّرْعَ وَالزَّيْتُونَ وَالنَّخِيلَ وَالْأَعْنَابَ وَمِن كُلِّ الثَّمَرَاتِ ۗ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَآيَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: yunbitu lakum bihi z-zarʿa wa-z-zaytūna wa-n-nakhīla wa-l-aʿnāba wa-min kulli th-thamarāti inna fī dhālika la-āyatan li-qawmin yatafakkarūna
versets.trad: With it He brings forth for you the crops, the olives, the date palms, the vines, and all manner of fruits. In that is truly a sign for a people who reflect.
themes: a sign for those who reflect
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī dwells on the singular “sign” (āya) here against the plural “signs” later: He said “signs for a people who reason” in the plural, because one who reaches gnosis attains a multitude of sciences; for the knower of his Lord there are many signs and proofs, since the proof of one matter differs from the proof of another. By one proof the manner of inquiry is known; by many proofs one becomes a knower of his Lord.

mots.racine: ز ر ع / ز ي ت

versets.ar: وَسَخَّرَ لَكُمُ اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهَارَ وَالشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ ۖ وَالنُّجُومُ مُسَخَّرَاتٌ بِأَمْرِهِ ۗ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَآيَاتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-sakhkhara lakumu l-layla wa-n-nahāra wa-sh-shamsa wa-l-qamara wa-n-nujūmu musakhkharātun bi-amrihi inna fī dhālika la-āyātin li-qawmin yaʿqilūna
versets.trad: He has subjected to you the night and the day, the sun and the moon; and the stars are made subservient by His command. In that are truly signs for a people who reason.
versets.commentaire: Night and day are the vessels of action, and people differ in their deeds. Qushayrī: the granted one spends his time in obedience to his Lord, the forsaken in following his whim; the worshipper is at a prescribed duty or a sustained supererogation, the knower at his remembrance — while the masters of unity are snatched away from times and moments by the overwhelming of the states that come upon them. And inwardly: the stars of knowledge, the moons of gnosis, the suns of unity.

mots.racine: س خ ر / ل ي ل
ش م س / ق م ر
س خ ر / أ م ر

versets.ar: وَمَا ذَرَأَ لَكُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ مُخْتَلِفًا أَلْوَانُهُ ۗ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَآيَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يَذَّكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā dharaʾa lakum fī l-arḍi mukhtalifan alwānuhu inna fī dhālika la-āyatan li-qawmin yadhdhakkarūna
versets.trad: And whatever He has scattered for you on the earth of varied colors — in that is truly a sign for a people who remember.
themes: a sign for those who remember
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī moves from the outward bounty to the inward poverty of the elect: for some He created on the earth gardens and thickets, mansions and palaces, dwellings and homelands, and every kind of blessing; while others own not a bird's perch nor a hand's span of earth — no home holds them, no tie binds them — and these are the masters among men, the light of the Real.

mots.def: dharaʾa: to scatter, create in abundance.

mots.racine: خ ل ف / ل و ن

versets.ar: وَهُوَ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ الْبَحْرَ لِتَأْكُلُوا مِنْهُ لَحْمًا طَرِيًّا وَتَسْتَخْرِجُوا مِنْهُ حِلْيَةً تَلْبَسُونَهَا وَتَرَى الْفُلْكَ مَوَاخِرَ فِيهِ وَلِتَبْتَغُوا مِن فَضْلِهِ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-huwa lladhī sakhkhara l-baḥra li-taʾkulū minhu laḥman ṭariyyan wa-tastakhrijū minhu ḥilyatan talbasūnahā wa-tarā l-fulka mawākhira fīhi wa-li-tabtaghū min faḍlihi wa-laʿallakum tashkurūna
versets.trad: It is He who subjected the sea, that you may eat tender flesh from it and bring out from it ornaments you wear; and you see the ships cleaving through it, that you may seek of His bounty, and that perhaps you may give thanks.
versets.commentaire: Outwardly He subjected the sea, eased riding upon it in ships, and made accessible its pearls and the fish for food. Qushayrī adds an inward reading: He created kinds of seas — some are drowned in the seas of distraction, others in the seas of grief, others in the seas of diversion. Safety from the sea of distraction is in boarding the ship of trust (tawakkul); rescue from the sea of grief, in the ship of contentment (riḍā); safety from the sea of diversion, in the ship of remembrance (dhikr).

mots.racine: س خ ر / ب ح ر
ل ح م / ط ر و
ف ل ك / م خ ر

versets.ar: وَأَلْقَىٰ فِي الْأَرْضِ رَوَاسِيَ أَن تَمِيدَ بِكُمْ وَأَنْهَارًا وَسُبُلًا لَّعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-alqā fī l-arḍi rawāsiya an tamīda bikum wa-anhāran wa-subulan laʿallakum tahtadūna
versets.trad: And He cast firm mountains upon the earth, lest it sway with you, and rivers and paths, that perhaps you may be guided,
versets.commentaire: Outwardly the “anchors” (rawāsī) are the mountains; in allusion, Qushayrī says, they are the friends of God (awliyāʾ), who are the succor of creation — through them He shows mercy, through them He gives relief; among them are the substitutes (abdāl), the pillars (awtād), and the pole (quṭb). He cites the report: “The shaykh among his people is like the prophet among his community,” and the verse: “God would not punish them while you are among them.”

mots.racine: ل ق ي / ر س و
mots.sens: cast firm mountains on the earth

mots.racine: ن ه ر / س ب ل

versets.ar: وَعَلَامَاتٍ ۚ وَبِالنَّجْمِ هُمْ يَهْتَدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ʿalāmātin wa-bi-n-najmi hum yahtadūna
versets.trad: and landmarks; and by the stars they are guided.
versets.commentaire: The planets are the stars of the sky, and among them are missiles against the devils; the friends of God are stars upon the earth. Qushayrī: likewise the scholars, who are leaders in unity (tawḥīd) and missiles against the deniers. And he distinguishes: there are stars by which one is guided through the byways of this world, and stars by which one is guided to God Himself.

versets.ar: أَفَمَن يَخْلُقُ كَمَن لَّا يَخْلُقُ ۗ أَفَلَا تَذَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: a-fa-man yakhluqu ka-man lā yakhluqu a-fa-lā tadhakkarūna
versets.trad: Is then He who creates like one who does not create? Will you not then remember?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: this verse establishes the denial of any likeness between Him and His creation. The attributes of pre-eternity belong to God by right; what is of the marks of origination and the traits of the created, the Real is hallowed above all of it. The essence of the Eternal is not likened to the essences of the created, nor His attributes to theirs, nor His decree to theirs. The root of every misguidance is assimilation (tashbīh) — so vile that everyone disavows it.

mots.sens: like one who does not create

versets.ar: وَإِن تَعُدُّوا نِعْمَةَ اللَّهِ لَا تُحْصُوهَا ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَغَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-in taʿuddū niʿmata llāhi lā tuḥṣūhā inna llāha la-ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: And if you would count the favors of God, you could not number them. Indeed God is forgiving, merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the existing things you cannot number, for your knowledge falls short of them; and the favors of warding-off (niʿam al-dafʿ) are without end. He notes the human blindness: man perceives only the favors of bestowal, but the endless favors of warding-off he scarcely senses, and so he gives no thanks for them — and how many they are! God is forgiving and merciful, overlooking when you fail in gratitude and accepting your mere recognition.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ر ح م

versets.ar: وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مَا تُسِرُّونَ وَمَا تُعْلِنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-llāhu yaʿlamu mā tusirrūna wa-mā tuʿlinūna
versets.trad: And God knows what you conceal and what you reveal.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: what you conceal — of sincerity or of regard for creatures — nothing of it is hidden from Him; and what you reveal — of harmony or discord, of beneficence or rebellion. The verse, he says, holds dread for the people of slips and honor for the people of obedience: the gaze that sees all is at once warning and intimacy.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ لَا يَخْلُقُونَ شَيْئًا وَهُمْ يُخْلَقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna yadʿūna min dūni llāhi lā yakhluqūna shayʾan wa-hum yukhlaqūna
versets.trad: And those they invoke besides God create nothing, while they themselves are created;
versets.commentaire: He informs that creation is impossible from the idols, since they are themselves created; the verse shows that whatever bears the mark of being created cannot create. Qushayrī, taking creation as bringing-into-being, finds in the verse a proof bearing on the createdness of acts: whoever is touched by the trait of being originated cannot originate. And he adds the inner sense: whoever attaches his heart to a thing, fancying good or evil from it, has associated with God by his conjecture.

versets.ar: أَمْوَاتٌ غَيْرُ أَحْيَاءٍ ۖ وَمَا يَشْعُرُونَ أَيَّانَ يُبْعَثُونَ
versets.tr: amwātun ghayru aḥyāʾin wa-mā yashʿurūna ayyāna yubʿathūna
versets.trad: They are dead, not living, and they do not know when they will be raised.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: for one upon whom the trait of being brought-into-being is laid, bringing-into-being is impossible. Then he turns to the heart's unity: whoever attaches his heart to a thing and imagines from it good or harm has associated with God by his fancy; true unity is the stripping of the heart of any supposition — of any sliver of negation or affirmation — toward all creatures and created things.

versets.ar: إِلَٰهُكُمْ إِلَٰهٌ وَاحِدٌ ۚ فَالَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ قُلُوبُهُم مُّنكِرَةٌ وَهُم مُّسْتَكْبِرُونَ
versets.tr: ilāhukum ilāhun wāḥidun fa-lladhīna lā yuʾminūna bi-l-ākhirati qulūbuhum munkiratun wa-hum mustakbirūna
versets.trad: Your God is one God. As for those who do not believe in the hereafter, their hearts are in denial, and they are arrogant.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He has no partner in His essence, whether possible or necessary, no likeness and no associate. Whoever does not realize this decisively, with the detailed testimony of the proofs, is fallen into the abysses of association and far from the realities of unity. Of the deniers He says their hearts are in denial and they are arrogant — captive in the bondage of association and the veil of disbelief, yet the cause for one who desires gnosis is available and the proofs of creation are manifest.

mots.racine: أ ل ه / و ح د
ق ل ب / ن ك ر

versets.ar: لَا جَرَمَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا يُسِرُّونَ وَمَا يُعْلِنُونَ ۚ إِنَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ الْمُسْتَكْبِرِينَ
versets.tr: lā jarama anna llāha yaʿlamu mā yusirrūna wa-mā yuʿlinūna innahu lā yuḥibbu l-mustakbirīna
versets.trad: Without doubt, God knows what they conceal and what they reveal. Indeed He does not love the arrogant.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He will expose them and lay bare their hypocrisy, making plain to the believers their disbelief and discord. And from “He does not love the arrogant” he draws the implied address: He loves the humble and the lowly-hearted — and it suffices them as bounty that the Real announces His love for them.

mots.racine: س ر ر / ع ل ن
ح ب ب / ك ب ر

versets.ar: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُم مَّاذَا أَنزَلَ رَبُّكُمْ ۙ قَالُوا أَسَاطِيرُ الْأَوَّلِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā qīla lahum mādhā anzala rabbukum qālū asāṭīru l-awwalīna
versets.trad: And when it is said to them, “What has your Lord sent down?” they say, “Tales of the ancients!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the ill omen of their denial overtook them, so they persisted in turning from inquiry; their hearts hardened and would not incline to acknowledging the truth. They obscured matters for whoever questioned them and said: what Muḥammad has brought is among the fables of the foreigners — so they strayed and led astray.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / ر ب ب
mots.sens: what has your Lord sent down

mots.racine: س ط ر / أ و ل

versets.ar: لِيَحْمِلُوا أَوْزَارَهُمْ كَامِلَةً يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ۙ وَمِنْ أَوْزَارِ الَّذِينَ يُضِلُّونَهُم بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ ۗ أَلَا سَاءَ مَا يَزِرُونَ
versets.tr: li-yaḥmilū awzārahum kāmilatan yawma l-qiyāmati wa-min awzāri lladhīna yuḍillūnahum bi-ghayri ʿilmin alā sāʾa mā yazirūna
versets.trad: Let them bear their burdens in full on the Day of Resurrection, and a portion of the burdens of those they led astray without knowledge. How evil is the load they bear!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: since in this world they strove for other than God, their deeds were not made pure; and in the hereafter they bear their burdens with them. These are the ones who have lost in this world and the next. The weight of leading others astray is added to one's own.

mots.racine: ح م ل / و ز ر
ض ل ل / ع ل م
س و أ / و ز ر

versets.ar: قَدْ مَكَرَ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ فَأَتَى اللَّهُ بُنْيَانَهُم مِّنَ الْقَوَاعِدِ فَخَرَّ عَلَيْهِمُ السَّقْفُ مِن فَوْقِهِمْ وَأَتَاهُمُ الْعَذَابُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ
versets.tr: qad makara lladhīna min qablihim fa-atā llāhu bunyānahum mina l-qawāʿidi fa-kharra ʿalayhimu s-saqfu min fawqihim wa-atāhumu l-ʿadhābu min ḥaythu lā yashʿurūna
versets.trad: Those before them schemed, and God came at their edifice from the foundations, so the roof collapsed upon them from above, and the punishment came at them from where they did not perceive.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they were marked by scheming, so their scheming closed in upon them; they fell into what they had dug for others; deceived by the long respite, the punishment seized them from their place of safety; busied with their amusement, their sweetest living was embittered for them. The roof falling from above and the punishment from the unperceived quarter portray the certainty of the divine reckoning.

mots.racine: خ ر ر / س ق ف

versets.ar: ثُمَّ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ يُخْزِيهِمْ وَيَقُولُ أَيْنَ شُرَكَائِيَ الَّذِينَ كُنتُمْ تُشَاقُّونَ فِيهِمْ ۚ قَالَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ إِنَّ الْخِزْيَ الْيَوْمَ وَالسُّوءَ عَلَى الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: thumma yawma l-qiyāmati yukhzīhim wa-yaqūlu ayna shurakāʾiya lladhīna kuntum tushāqqūna fīhim qāla lladhīna ūtū l-ʿilma inna l-khizya l-yawma wa-s-sūʾa ʿalā l-kāfirīna
versets.trad: Then on the Day of Resurrection He will disgrace them and say, “Where are My associates over whom you used to dispute?” Those given knowledge will say, “Today disgrace and evil fall upon the disbelievers,”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: their affliction in this world is hastened, and the rest of it is before them. The regret of the bankrupt redoubles when he is called to account and sees the sum of his deeds. Of “those given knowledge” he says: He makes the deniers hear the believers' word and makes their truthfulness plain to all, while regret falls upon the ignorant among them. Today the believers' counsel is patience, for soon the veil is lifted.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ع ل م

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ تَتَوَفَّاهُمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ ظَالِمِي أَنفُسِهِمْ ۖ فَأَلْقَوُا السَّلَمَ مَا كُنَّا نَعْمَلُ مِن سُوءٍ ۚ بَلَىٰ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna tatawaffāhumu l-malāʾikatu ẓālimī anfusihim fa-alqawu s-salama mā kunnā naʿmalu min sūʾin balā inna llāha ʿalīmun bi-mā kuntum taʿmalūna
versets.trad: Those whom the angels take in death while they wrong themselves — then they offer submission, saying, “We were doing no evil.” Nay! Indeed God is fully aware of what you used to do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī glosses each phrase: “wronging themselves” by committing acts of disobedience — they are the disbelievers; “they offered submission” — they yielded and surrendered to God's judgment; “we were doing no evil” — they denied and disowned the transgressions they had committed. So the angels answer: nay, God knows fully. Likewise, he adds, those whose souls harden by turning from obedience fall into panic and pleading at death, yet will not confess the details of their deeds.

mots.racine: و ف ي / م ل ك
mots.sens: the angels take them in death

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ن ف س
ل ق ي / س ل م

versets.ar: فَادْخُلُوا أَبْوَابَ جَهَنَّمَ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا ۖ فَلَبِئْسَ مَثْوَى الْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ
versets.tr: fa-dkhulū abwāba jahannama khālidīna fīhā fa-la-biʾsa mathwā l-mutakabbirīna
versets.trad: So enter the gates of Hell, abiding therein forever! How evil is the abode of the arrogant!
versets.commentaire: Continuing the scene of the wrongdoers' death, Qushayrī describes how those who hardened against obedience refuse, even at the end, to confess their wrongs to people in what concerns satisfying their adversaries; then God holds them to account for great and small, for the date-thread and the pit, and they remain forever in the consequence of what they amassed, for its ill omen clings to them in their hereafter.

mots.racine: د خ ل / ج ه ن م
ث و ي / ك ب ر

versets.ar: وَقِيلَ لِلَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا مَاذَا أَنزَلَ رَبُّكُمْ ۚ قَالُوا خَيْرًا ۗ لِّلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا فِي هَٰذِهِ الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةٌ ۚ وَلَدَارُ الْآخِرَةِ خَيْرٌ ۚ وَلَنِعْمَ دَارُ الْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qīla li-lladhīna ttaqaw mādhā anzala rabbukum qālū khayran li-lladhīna aḥsanū fī hādhihi d-dunyā ḥasanatun wa-la-dāru l-ākhirati khayrun wa-la-niʿma dāru l-muttaqīna
versets.trad: And it is said to those who feared God, “What has your Lord sent down?” They say, “Good!” For those who do good there is good in this world, but the abode of the hereafter is better. How excellent is the home of the godfearing!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī enumerates what the “good in this world” may be for those who do good: the sweetness of obedience through the purity of the moment; an increase of divine enabling in their deeds and states; the gift of being steadied in their excellence; their being brought to the ranks of the great; or the blessings that pass from them to others through guidance. Then: the abode of the hereafter is better, for what is in it abides without fear of passing — in this world is witnessing, in the hereafter direct vision.

mots.racine: ق و ل / خ ي ر
د و ر / أ خ ر
mots.sens: the home of the hereafter is better

versets.ar: جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ يَدْخُلُونَهَا تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ ۖ لَهُمْ فِيهَا مَا يَشَاءُونَ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يَجْزِي اللَّهُ الْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: jannātu ʿadnin yadkhulūnahā tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru lahum fīhā mā yashāʾūna ka-dhālika yajzī llāhu l-muttaqīna
versets.trad: Gardens of Eden which they enter, beneath which rivers flow; therein they have whatever they wish. Thus does God reward the godfearing,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī observes that just as longings and aspirations differ in this world, so they differ in the hereafter: one seeker is content with the Garden by merely entering it; another is not content with the Garden short of beholding the Lord of the Garden. Thus “they have whatever they wish”: whoever wishes for the abiding of his vision and the everlasting hearing of His address — they have that, and with Us is yet more, which has never crossed any mind.

mots.racine: ج ن ن / ع د ن
mots.def: jannat ʿadn: gardens of perpetual abode.

mots.racine: ج ز ي / و ق ي

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ تَتَوَفَّاهُمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ طَيِّبِينَ ۙ يَقُولُونَ سَلَامٌ عَلَيْكُمُ ادْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna tatawaffāhumu l-malāʾikatu ṭayyibīna yaqūlūna salāmun ʿalaykumu dkhulū l-jannata bi-mā kuntum taʿmalūna
versets.trad: Those whom the angels take in death in a state of goodness, saying, “Peace be upon you! Enter Paradise for what you used to do.”
versets.commentaire: Their souls are taken pure. Qushayrī marvels at the diversity of what makes hearts good at death: one's moment is made good because his sins are forgiven and his faults veiled; another's, because his Beloved greets him; another's, because his sought-for did not escape him; another rejoices that he is safe from the loss of his state; another that he has reached His grace; another His beauty; and another is singled out for the unveiling of His majesty — each people has known its drinking-place.

mots.racine: د خ ل / ج ن ن

versets.ar: هَلْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّا أَن تَأْتِيَهُمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ أَوْ يَأْتِيَ أَمْرُ رَبِّكَ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ فَعَلَ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ وَمَا ظَلَمَهُمُ اللَّهُ وَلَٰكِن كَانُوا أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: hal yanẓurūna illā an taʾtiyahumu l-malāʾikatu aw yaʾtiya amru rabbika ka-dhālika faʿala lladhīna min qablihim wa-mā ẓalamahumu llāhu wa-lākin kānū anfusahum yaẓlimūna
versets.trad: Do they await only that the angels should come to them, or the command of your Lord should come to pass? Thus did those before them. God did not wrong them, but they wronged themselves.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: these people await the coming of the angel because they did not know him nor believe in his being; yet because they sought to hasten it, holding the messengers untruthful, and walked the path of their predecessors, they were dealt with as their forebears were. None of this was injustice from God, for He disposes of His dominion with no judge above Him.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / أ م ر
mots.sens: the command of your Lord comes

mots.racine: ن ف س / ظ ل م

versets.ar: فَأَصَابَهُمْ سَيِّئَاتُ مَا عَمِلُوا وَحَاقَ بِهِم مَّا كَانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِئُونَ
versets.tr: fa-aṣābahum sayyiʾātu mā ʿamilū wa-ḥāqa bihim mā kānū bihi yastahziʾūna
versets.trad: So the evils of what they did struck them, and what they mocked encompassed them on every side.
versets.commentaire: In the flow of the preceding, Qushayrī sees the just recompense: the evil deeds they committed returned upon them, and the very thing they ridiculed closed in around them. The mockery of revelation becomes the encircling chastisement; the scoffer is hemmed in by what he scorned.

mots.racine: ص و ب / س و أ
mots.def: aṣāba: to befall; sayyiʾa: evil deed.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا لَوْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ مَا عَبَدْنَا مِن دُونِهِ مِن شَيْءٍ نَّحْنُ وَلَا آبَاؤُنَا وَلَا حَرَّمْنَا مِن دُونِهِ مِن شَيْءٍ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ فَعَلَ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ فَهَلْ عَلَى الرُّسُلِ إِلَّا الْبَلَاغُ الْمُبِينُ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lladhīna ashrakū law shāʾa llāhu mā ʿabadnā min dūnihi min shayʾin naḥnu wa-lā ābāʾunā wa-lā ḥarramnā min dūnihi min shayʾin ka-dhālika faʿala lladhīna min qablihim fa-hal ʿalā r-rusuli illā l-balāghu l-mubīnu
versets.trad: The associators say, “Had God willed, we would not have worshipped anything besides Him, neither we nor our fathers, nor would we have forbidden anything apart from Him.” Thus did those before them. Is there upon the messengers but the clear delivery of the message?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: their intentions were corrupt in what they said, by way of denial and mockery; the darkness of their ignorance and rejection overcame their speech, and the falseness of their states was laid bare. Their saying “had God willed” resembles their other word, “shall we feed one whom, if God willed, He would feed?” — and there is no dispute that had God willed to feed them, it would be so. To the messengers belongs only the clear delivery.

mots.sens: we would not have worshipped besides Him

mots.racine: ب ل غ / ب ي ن

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ بَعَثْنَا فِي كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ رَّسُولًا أَنِ اعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ وَاجْتَنِبُوا الطَّاغُوتَ ۖ فَمِنْهُم مَّنْ هَدَى اللَّهُ وَمِنْهُم مَّنْ حَقَّتْ عَلَيْهِ الضَّلَالَةُ ۚ فَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَانظُرُوا كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الْمُكَذِّبِينَ
versets.tr: wa-la-qad baʿathnā fī kulli ummatin rasūlan ani ʿbudū llāha wa-jtanibū ṭ-ṭāghūta fa-minhum man hadā llāhu wa-minhum man ḥaqqat ʿalayhi ḍ-ḍalālatu fa-sīrū fī l-arḍi fa-nẓurū kayfa kāna ʿāqibatu l-mukadhdhibīna
versets.trad: We sent to every community a messenger, saying, “Worship God and shun the false idols (the Ṭāghūt).” Among them were those whom God guided, and others upon whom misguidance was confirmed. So travel through the earth and see what was the end of those who cried lies.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He left no age void of revelation, as a clarification of His proof; but He divided them in His prior decree — one party He guided, and a party He veiled and blinded. The summons to worship God and shun false gods was universal; the difference of outcomes points back to the hidden apportionment.

mots.racine: ب ع ث / أ م م / ر س ل
mots.sens: We sent in every community a messenger

mots.racine: ج ن ب / ط غ ي
ع ق ب / ك ذ ب

versets.ar: إِن تَحْرِصْ عَلَىٰ هُدَاهُمْ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِي مَن يُضِلُّ ۖ وَمَا لَهُم مِّن نَّاصِرِينَ
versets.tr: in taḥriṣ ʿalā hudāhum fa-inna llāha lā yahdī man yuḍillu wa-mā lahum min nāṣirīna
versets.trad: Even if you long for their guidance, God does not guide those He lets stray, and they will have no helpers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He held him to the bound of servanthood in his desire for their guidance and for their knowing the realities of Lordship, saying: even though by Our command you are eager for their guidance, whoever has been apportioned misguidance — nothing befalls him but what was apportioned him. And he adds: whomever I have clothed in the robe of misguidance, no means and no intercession can strip it from him.

mots.racine: ح ر ص / ه د ي
ه د ي / ض ل ل
mots.sens: guides not whom He lets stray

versets.ar: وَأَقْسَمُوا بِاللَّهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمَانِهِمْ ۙ لَا يَبْعَثُ اللَّهُ مَن يَمُوتُ ۚ بَلَىٰ وَعْدًا عَلَيْهِ حَقًّا وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-aqsamū bi-llāhi jahda aymānihim lā yabʿathu llāhu man yamūtu balā waʿdan ʿalayhi ḥaqqan wa-lākinna akthara n-nāsi lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: They swear by God their most solemn oaths that God will not raise one who dies. Nay! It is a promise binding upon Him in truth, but most people do not know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the oath confirms the report, but the liar's oath weakens his word, for the more he adds to his denial of God, the more the heart recoils from his speech. The very vehemence of their swearing betrays the falsehood of their claim.

mots.racine: ج ه د / ي م ن
و ع د / ح ق ق

versets.ar: لِيُبَيِّنَ لَهُمُ الَّذِي يَخْتَلِفُونَ فِيهِ وَلِيَعْلَمَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا كَاذِبِينَ
versets.tr: li-yubayyina lahumu lladhī yakhtalifūna fīhi wa-li-yaʿlama lladhīna kafarū annahum kānū kādhibīna
versets.trad: That He may make clear to them that wherein they differ, and that the disbelievers may know they were liars.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when God makes plain in the hereafter the truth of what revelation brought, by unveiling the unseen, the disgrace of the deniers increases — and in that there is an increase of their punishment. The clarification that consoles the believer is exposure for the one who lied.

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا قَوْلُنَا لِشَيْءٍ إِذَا أَرَدْنَاهُ أَن نَّقُولَ لَهُ كُن فَيَكُونُ
versets.tr: innamā qawlunā li-shayʾin idhā aradnāhu an naqūla lahu kun fa-yakūnu
versets.trad: Our word to a thing, when We will it, is only to say to it “Be,” and it is.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: by hearing comes the knowing that His word attaches to what He does. Some read it as: nothing He wills is difficult for Him. He notes the dogma: the verse shows that His word is not created, for were it created it would itself need a “Be,” and that command a further command, leading to an endless regress. The Word is uncreated, the command effortless.

mots.racine: ق و ل / ش ي أ

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ هَاجَرُوا فِي اللَّهِ مِن بَعْدِ مَا ظُلِمُوا لَنُبَوِّئَنَّهُمْ فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً ۖ وَلَأَجْرُ الْآخِرَةِ أَكْبَرُ ۚ لَوْ كَانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna hājarū fī llāhi min baʿdi mā ẓulimū la-nubawwiʾannahum fī d-dunyā ḥasanatan wa-la-ajru l-ākhirati akbaru law kānū yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: Those who emigrated for God after they had been wronged — We shall surely settle them in a goodly place in this world, and the reward of the hereafter is greater still, if only they knew,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī interiorizes the hijra: whoever emigrates from the homelands of evil — for God — God grants him, in the neighborhood of His friends, what aids the purity of his moment; whoever forsakes the homelands of heedlessness, God enables to witness union; whoever leaves the company of creatures and cuts off with his heart to Him through constant remembrance — as the report says, “I am the companion of whoever remembers Me.” And he adds: the heart is wronged by the soul that summons it to its desires; when it emigrates from the soul, God bequeaths it the soul's own homelands until they yield to obedience.

mots.racine: أ ج ر / أ خ ر / ك ب ر
mots.sens: the reward of the hereafter is greater

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا وَعَلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna ṣabarū wa-ʿalā rabbihim yatawakkalūna
versets.trad: those who were patient and put their trust in their Lord.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives a cascade of definitions: patience (ṣabr) is standing firm according to the flow of the decree, and trust (tawakkul) is taking shelter in God with fair hope. They were patient in the present and trusting in God for the fulfillment of their hopes. Patience is the sipping of the cups of destiny; trust is confidence in God against the dreaded thing. They gain strength to be patient only through what they have realized of trust.

versets.ar: وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَا مِن قَبْلِكَ إِلَّا رِجَالًا نُّوحِي إِلَيْهِمْ ۚ فَاسْأَلُوا أَهْلَ الذِّكْرِ إِن كُنتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā arsalnā min qablika illā rijālan nūḥī ilayhim fa-sʾalū ahla dh-dhikri in kuntum lā taʿlamūna
versets.trad: We sent before you none but men to whom We gave revelation; so ask the people of the Reminder if you do not know.
versets.commentaire: They marveled that messengers should be human; He told them all messengers were human. Qushayrī expounds “the people of the Reminder” (ahl al-dhikr) as the scholars, who differ in kind: for rulings, recourse is to the jurists; for the science of wayfaring on the path to God, recourse is to the knowers of God (ʿārifūn). The jurist signs on God's behalf in matters of command and prohibition; the gnostic speaks, in the courtesies of seeking and the rulings of aspiration, from God.

mots.racine: ر ج ل / و ح ي
أ ه ل / ذ ك ر

versets.ar: بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ وَالزُّبُرِ ۗ وَأَنزَلْنَا إِلَيْكَ الذِّكْرَ لِتُبَيِّنَ لِلنَّاسِ مَا نُزِّلَ إِلَيْهِمْ وَلَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: bi-l-bayyināti wa-z-zuburi wa-anzalnā ilayka dh-dhikra li-tubayyina li-n-nāsi mā nuzzila ilayhim wa-laʿallahum yatafakkarūna
versets.trad: With clear proofs and scriptures. And We sent down to you the Reminder, that you may make clear to people what was sent down to them, and that perhaps they may reflect.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the clarification is upon you — you are the intermediary between Us and them, the trustee over Our revelation. The Prophet is the channel through which the descended Reminder is unfolded to humankind, that they may reflect.

mots.racine: ب ي ن / ز ب ر
ن ز ل / ذ ك ر
mots.sens: We sent down to you the Reminder

mots.racine: ب ي ن / ن و س
mots.sens: that you make clear to people

versets.ar: أَفَأَمِنَ الَّذِينَ مَكَرُوا السَّيِّئَاتِ أَن يَخْسِفَ اللَّهُ بِهِمُ الْأَرْضَ أَوْ يَأْتِيَهُمُ الْعَذَابُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ
versets.tr: a-fa-amina lladhīna makarū s-sayyiʾāti an yakhsifa llāhu bihimu l-arḍa aw yaʾtiyahumu l-ʿadhābu min ḥaythu lā yashʿurūna
versets.trad: Do those who plot evil deeds feel secure that God will not cause the earth to swallow them, or that the punishment will not come upon them from where they do not perceive,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the servant in all his states is exposed to the arrows of destiny; he should sense fear with every breath of being struck by them, and never feel secure from God's stratagem at any time. He warns against the false repose of those upon whom God has accustomed His favors, citing: “O sleeper of the night, delighted by its beginning — calamities may knock at dawn.”

mots.racine: م ك ر / س و أ
خ س ف / أ ر ض
mots.sens: God makes the earth swallow them

versets.ar: أَوْ يَأْخُذَهُمْ فِي تَقَلُّبِهِمْ فَمَا هُم بِمُعْجِزِينَ
versets.tr: aw yaʾkhudhahum fī taqallubihim fa-mā hum bi-muʿjizīna
versets.trad: or that He will seize them in the midst of their going to and fro, so they cannot escape,
versets.commentaire: In the same vein, Qushayrī holds that the servant is never beyond the reach of the decree: God may seize the plotters in the very midst of their comings and goings, and they have no power to thwart Him. No bustle of activity shields one from the sudden grasp.

mots.sens: in their going to and fro

versets.ar: أَوْ يَأْخُذَهُمْ عَلَىٰ تَخَوُّفٍ فَإِنَّ رَبَّكُمْ لَرَءُوفٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: aw yaʾkhudhahum ʿalā takhawwufin fa-inna rabbakum la-raʾūfun raḥīmun
versets.trad: or that He will seize them in a state of dread? Yet your Lord is most kind, compassionate!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī balances the warning with mercy: even amid the threat of being seized, “your Lord is most kind, compassionate.” The same Hand that can seize is the Hand of tenderness — fear and hope held together, for the dread is meant to draw the heart back, not to crush it.

mots.racine: ر أ ف / ر ح م

versets.ar: أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا إِلَىٰ مَا خَلَقَ اللَّهُ مِن شَيْءٍ يَتَفَيَّأُ ظِلَالُهُ عَنِ الْيَمِينِ وَالشَّمَائِلِ سُجَّدًا لِّلَّهِ وَهُمْ دَاخِرُونَ
versets.tr: a-wa-lam yaraw ilā mā khalaqa llāhu min shayʾin yatafayyaʾu ẓilāluhu ʿani l-yamīni wa-sh-shamāʾili sujjadan li-llāhi wa-hum dākhirūna
versets.trad: Have they not seen what God has created — its shadows incline to the right and the left, prostrating to God, while they are humble?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: every created thing, whether substance or trace, of stone or clay or dust, is — from the standpoint of proof — prostrate to God, and from the standpoint of clear evidence, a witness to His oneness. The inclining shadow is the silent worship of all that exists.

mots.racine: ف ي أ / ظ ل ل
mots.def: tafayyaʾa: to shift (of shadow); ẓill: shade.

versets.ar: وَلِلَّهِ يَسْجُدُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ مِن دَابَّةٍ وَالْمَلَائِكَةُ وَهُمْ لَا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-li-llāhi yasjudu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi min dābbatin wa-l-malāʾikatu wa-hum lā yastakbirūna
versets.trad: To God prostrates whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth of living creatures, and the angels too, and they are not arrogant.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two prostrations: this is the prostration of testimony, not the prostration of worship; so even if some withhold the worship-prostration through their denial, every part of them bears witness, by way of proof and evidence, to His oneness. The whole cosmos confesses, willing or not.

mots.racine: س ج د / س م و
mots.sens: prostrates what is in the heavens

versets.ar: يَخَافُونَ رَبَّهُم مِّن فَوْقِهِمْ وَيَفْعَلُونَ مَا يُؤْمَرُونَ
versets.tr: yakhāfūna rabbahum min fawqihim wa-yafʿalūna mā yuʾmarūna
versets.trad: They fear their Lord above them, and they do what they are commanded.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they fear that God should send down upon them a punishment from above their heads; “they do what they are commanded” — they do not disobey Him nor swerve from His obedience. And he draws a maxim: the best thing for the servant in this world and the next is fear, for it keeps him from the slip and carries him to obedience.

mots.racine: خ و ف / ر ب ب
ف ع ل / أ م ر
mots.sens: they do what they are commanded

versets.ar: وَقَالَ اللَّهُ لَا تَتَّخِذُوا إِلَٰهَيْنِ اثْنَيْنِ ۖ إِنَّمَا هُوَ إِلَٰهٌ وَاحِدٌ ۖ فَإِيَّايَ فَارْهَبُونِ
versets.tr: wa-qāla llāhu lā tattakhidhū ilāhayni thnayni innamā huwa ilāhun wāḥidun fa-iyyāya fa-rhabūni
versets.trad: And God said: “Do not take two gods. He is but one God, so fear Me alone!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the need to affirm a single Maker is compelling; what is added beyond the One is, in possibility, all on a par. And he sums it tersely: the affirmation of the One is a necessity, and the power of two would be bounded. Two cannot share the absolute; only the One can be feared without rival.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / أ ل ه
أ ل ه / و ح د

versets.ar: وَلَهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَلَهُ الدِّينُ وَاصِبًا ۚ أَفَغَيْرَ اللَّهِ تَتَّقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lahu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-lahu d-dīnu wāṣiban a-fa-ghayra llāhi tattaqūna
versets.trad: To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth, and to Him is owed perpetual worship. Will you then fear other than God?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to Him belongs the religion purely, the religion always, the religion firmly; obedience to Him is obligatory. So fear none but Him, obey His law against your whim, worship Him alone, and answer Him in joy and in adversity. Perpetual worship admits no rival object of dread.

mots.sens: to Him belongs what is in the heavens

mots.racine: د ي ن / و ص ب
غ ي ر / و ق ي
mots.sens: do you fear other than God

versets.ar: وَمَا بِكُم مِّن نِّعْمَةٍ فَمِنَ اللَّهِ ۖ ثُمَّ إِذَا مَسَّكُمُ الضُّرُّ فَإِلَيْهِ تَجْأَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā bikum min niʿmatin fa-mina llāhi thumma idhā massakumu ḍ-ḍurru fa-ilayhi tajʾarūna
versets.trad: Whatever blessing you have is from God; then, when harm touches you, to Him you cry for help.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives a deep definition of blessing: the true blessing is what draws the servant near to the Real; what brings forgetfulness, insolence, heedlessness and disobedience is more fittingly a trial. Yet he allows the broader sense — whatever benefits the servant or wards off harm is, on the soundest view, a blessing, religious or worldly; the servant is commanded to give thanks in every state, though most give thanks only for the blessings of bestowal, while “few of My servants are grateful” in every state.

mots.racine: م س س / ض ر ر

versets.ar: ثُمَّ إِذَا كَشَفَ الضُّرَّ عَنكُمْ إِذَا فَرِيقٌ مِّنكُم بِرَبِّهِمْ يُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: thumma idhā kashafa ḍ-ḍurra ʿankum idhā farīqun minkum bi-rabbihim yushrikūna
versets.trad: Then, when He removes the harm from you, behold, a party of you associate others with their Lord,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the address is general, and “a party of you” because they are among the addressed. He likens them to the rescued who forget the storm: once relief comes, the servant becomes as if no evil had touched him, as if no grief had befallen him — and a faction turns again to associating partners, the very ones who had cried out to Him alone in their distress.

mots.racine: ك ش ف / ض ر ر
mots.sens: He removed the harm from you

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ش ر ك

versets.ar: لِيَكْفُرُوا بِمَا آتَيْنَاهُمْ ۚ فَتَمَتَّعُوا ۖ فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: li-yakfurū bi-mā ātaynāhum fa-tamattaʿū fa-sawfa taʿlamūna
versets.trad: so as to deny what We have given them. Enjoy yourselves, then, for soon you will know!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads it as a threat: they will regret when regret avails nothing, and offer excuses when no excuse is accepted; whoever sows evil will reap only the requital of his deed. The brief enjoyment of denial ends in a knowing that comes too late.

versets.ar: وَيَجْعَلُونَ لِمَا لَا يَعْلَمُونَ نَصِيبًا مِّمَّا رَزَقْنَاهُمْ ۗ تَاللَّهِ لَتُسْأَلُنَّ عَمَّا كُنتُمْ تَفْتَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yajʿalūna li-mā lā yaʿlamūna naṣīban mimmā razaqnāhum ta-llāhi la-tusʾalunna ʿammā kuntum taftarūna
versets.trad: They assign to what they do not know a portion of what We have provided them. By God, you will surely be questioned about what you used to invent!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they assign to what they do not know — namely their idols, which have no claim to knowledge — a portion of their provision, saying, “This is for them and this for our associates.” “By God” — He swears that they will meet the requital of their deed. The portion given to the unknown is a theft from the Provider's gift.

mots.sens: to what they do not know

versets.ar: وَيَجْعَلُونَ لِلَّهِ الْبَنَاتِ سُبْحَانَهُ ۙ وَلَهُم مَّا يَشْتَهُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yajʿalūna li-llāhi l-banāti subḥānahu wa-lahum mā yashtahūna
versets.trad: And they assign daughters to God — glory be to Him! — while for themselves they keep what they desire.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: from the excess of their ignorance they described the Worshipped as having offspring; then God increased their forsaking until they said: the angels are God's daughters. They detested daughters, so they were content for God with what they would not accept for themselves. And he joins to them in blame everyone who prefers the lot of his own soul over the right of his Master.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا بُشِّرَ أَحَدُهُم بِالْأُنثَىٰ ظَلَّ وَجْهُهُ مُسْوَدًّا وَهُوَ كَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-idhā bushshira aḥaduhum bi-l-unthā ẓalla wajhuhu muswaddan wa-huwa kaẓīmun
versets.trad: When one of them is given the news of a daughter, his face darkens and he is choked with grief;
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the vision of creatures overcame them and bewilderment seized them, so they raged against daughters because of what attaches to them at their marriage. These are the fruits of dwelling in the homelands of dispersion and absence from the witnessing of the Reality. The darkening face betrays a heart estranged from the divine wisdom in things.

mots.racine: ب ش ر / أ ن ث
و ج ه / س و د

versets.ar: يَتَوَارَىٰ مِنَ الْقَوْمِ مِن سُوءِ مَا بُشِّرَ بِهِ ۚ أَيُمْسِكُهُ عَلَىٰ هُونٍ أَمْ يَدُسُّهُ فِي التُّرَابِ ۗ أَلَا سَاءَ مَا يَحْكُمُونَ
versets.tr: yatawārā mina l-qawmi min sūʾi mā bushshira bihi a-yumsikuhu ʿalā hūnin am yadussuhu fī t-turābi alā sāʾa mā yaḥkumūna
versets.trad: He hides from people because of the bad news he has received: shall he keep her in humiliation, or bury her in the dust? How evil is the judgment they make!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “shall he keep her in humiliation” — hold the newborn, if female, in lowliness — “or bury her in the dust” to die? That harshness in their states, born of the hardness of their hearts, made the punishment severer. Their excessive rage at one who had no fault among their children made them dwellers of the lowest depths of Hell; their moment was fouled and desolation overcame them.

mots.racine: و ر ي / ق و م
م س ك / ه و ن
د س س / ت ر ب

versets.ar: لِلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ مَثَلُ السَّوْءِ ۖ وَلِلَّهِ الْمَثَلُ الْأَعْلَىٰ ۚ وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: li-lladhīna lā yuʾminūna bi-l-ākhirati mathalu s-sawʾi wa-li-llāhi l-mathalu l-aʿlā wa-huwa l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: To those who do not believe in the hereafter belongs the likeness of evil, while to God belongs the loftiest likeness. He is the Mighty, the Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the likeness of evil is for the deniers who rejected His oneness; to God belong the attributes of majesty and the qualities of might. Whoever knows Him by the description of divinity, his felicity is complete in both worlds, his rest is hastened, his secret is forever refreshed in the meadows of His gnosis, and his spirit forever exults in the surging of its ecstasy. The lofty likeness, the marginal note adds, is found not among created things but in Names like al-Aḥad, the One.

mots.racine: م ث ل / س و أ
م ث ل / ع ل و
ع ز ز / ح ك م

versets.ar: وَلَوْ يُؤَاخِذُ اللَّهُ النَّاسَ بِظُلْمِهِم مَّا تَرَكَ عَلَيْهَا مِن دَابَّةٍ وَلَٰكِن يُؤَخِّرُهُمْ إِلَىٰ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۖ فَإِذَا جَاءَ أَجَلُهُمْ لَا يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ سَاعَةً ۖ وَلَا يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-law yuʾākhidhu llāhu n-nāsa bi-ẓulmihim mā taraka ʿalayhā min dābbatin wa-lākin yuʾakhkhiruhum ilā ajalin musamman fa-idhā jāʾa ajaluhum lā yastaʾkhirūna sāʿatan wa-lā yastaqdimūna
versets.trad: If God were to take people to task for their wrongdoing, He would leave no creature upon it; but He defers them to an appointed term, and when their term comes they cannot delay it by an hour, nor bring it forward.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: were He to deal with them at once as they deserve, extirpation would befall them; but the decree preceded to grant them respite, and they will meet the consequence of their deeds in their final outcome. The forbearance of God is a respite, not a pardon, bounded by a term that none can move.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ظ ل م
mots.sens: take people to task for their wrong

mots.racine: أ ج ل / س م و
أ خ ر / س و ع

versets.ar: وَيَجْعَلُونَ لِلَّهِ مَا يَكْرَهُونَ وَتَصِفُ أَلْسِنَتُهُمُ الْكَذِبَ أَنَّ لَهُمُ الْحُسْنَىٰ ۚ لَا جَرَمَ أَنَّ لَهُمُ النَّارَ وَأَنَّهُم مُّفْرَطُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yajʿalūna li-llāhi mā yakrahūna wa-taṣifu alsinatuhumu l-kadhiba anna lahumu l-ḥusnā lā jarama anna lahumu n-nāra wa-annahum mufraṭūna
versets.trad: They assign to God what they themselves dislike, and their tongues utter the lie that the best shall be theirs. Without doubt, theirs is the Fire, and they will be abandoned to it first.
themes: the lie of the best portion
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they were deceived when life grew soft for them, fancying they would be saved and would encompass what they hoped; the ugliness of their traits seemed fair in their eyes. But on the day the veil is lifted, they will bite the fingertips of disappointment with the back teeth of regret — no plea will be heard from them, and no mercy will attach to any of them.

mots.racine: و ص ف / ل س ن / ك ذ ب

versets.ar: تَاللَّهِ لَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا إِلَىٰ أُمَمٍ مِّن قَبْلِكَ فَزَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الشَّيْطَانُ أَعْمَالَهُمْ فَهُوَ وَلِيُّهُمُ الْيَوْمَ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: ta-llāhi la-qad arsalnā ilā umamin min qablika fa-zayyana lahumu sh-shayṭānu aʿmālahum fa-huwa waliyyuhumu l-yawma wa-lahum ʿadhābun alīmun
versets.trad: By God, We sent messengers to communities before you, but Satan made their deeds seem fair to them. He is their patron today, and theirs is a painful punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads this as consolation for the Prophet: He told that the communities before him walked in misguidance and threaded the string of ignorance, as did his own people; yet God was not powerless over them. As Satan adorned the deeds of the former community and was their patron, so he is the patron of these. As for the believers, God is their patron; and the disbelievers have no patron at all.

mots.racine: ر س ل / أ م م
ز ي ن / ش ط ن

versets.ar: وَمَا أَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْكَ الْكِتَابَ إِلَّا لِتُبَيِّنَ لَهُمُ الَّذِي اخْتَلَفُوا فِيهِ ۙ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā anzalnā ʿalayka l-kitāba illā li-tubayyina lahumu lladhī khtalafū fīhi wa-hudan wa-raḥmatan li-qawmin yuʾminūna
versets.trad: We sent down the Book to you only that you might make clear to them that wherein they differed, and as guidance and mercy for a people who believe.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī addresses the Prophet: you are the intermediary between Us and Our friends; to you belong the loftiest proof and the fullest light; you convey from Us and deliver of Us — you are a mercy We sent to Our friends. Whoever follows you is guided; whoever disobeys you strives for his own ruin.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / ك ت ب
mots.sens: We sent down to you the Book

mots.racine: ه د ي / ر ح م

versets.ar: وَاللَّهُ أَنزَلَ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مَاءً فَأَحْيَا بِهِ الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا ۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَآيَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يَسْمَعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-llāhu anzala mina s-samāʾi māʾan fa-aḥyā bihi l-arḍa baʿda mawtihā inna fī dhālika la-āyatan li-qawmin yasmaʿūna
versets.trad: God sent down water from the sky and revived with it the earth after its death. In that is truly a sign for a people who listen.
themes: a sign for those who listen
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds an inner rain: with the water of enabling He revived the hearts of the worshippers, so they inclined to concord; with the water of realization He revived the spirits of the knowers, so they breathed upon the carpet of union; with the water of stripping-bare He revived the secrets of the unifiers, so they were freed from the bondage of traces and singled out by the realities of connection.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / م و ه
mots.sens: sent down water from the sky

mots.racine: ح ي ي / أ ر ض

versets.ar: وَإِنَّ لَكُمْ فِي الْأَنْعَامِ لَعِبْرَةً ۖ نُّسْقِيكُم مِّمَّا فِي بُطُونِهِ مِن بَيْنِ فَرْثٍ وَدَمٍ لَّبَنًا خَالِصًا سَائِغًا لِّلشَّارِبِينَ
versets.tr: wa-inna lakum fī l-anʿāmi la-ʿibratan nusqīkum mimmā fī buṭūnihi min bayni farthin wa-damin labanan khāliṣan sāʾighan li-sh-shāribīna
versets.trad: In the cattle is truly a lesson for you: We give you to drink, from what is in their bellies between chyme and blood, pure milk, palatable to those who drink it.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī marvels at the wonder of power: the wondrous thing He brought forth — milk, with its purity, taste, and benefit — from between dung and blood; that is the ordaining of the Mighty, the Knowing. And he draws the lesson: He who can preserve milk pure between filth and blood can preserve gnosis intact amid the desolations of the slip in its various forms.

mots.racine: ف ر ث / د م و
ل ب ن / خ ل ص / س و غ
mots.def: laban: milk; khāliṣ: pure; sāʾigh: easy to swallow.

versets.ar: وَمِن ثَمَرَاتِ النَّخِيلِ وَالْأَعْنَابِ تَتَّخِذُونَ مِنْهُ سَكَرًا وَرِزْقًا حَسَنًا ۗ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَآيَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-min thamarāti n-nakhīli wa-l-aʿnābi tattakhidhūna minhu sakaran wa-rizqan ḥasanan inna fī dhālika la-āyatan li-qawmin yaʿqilūna
versets.trad: And from the fruits of the date palm and the vine you take an intoxicating drink and a goodly provision. In that is truly a sign for a people who reason.
themes: the fruit of palm and vine
versets.commentaire: This verse precedes the prohibition of wine. Qushayrī dwells on the “goodly provision” (rizq ḥasan): it is the lawful; it is said to be what reaches you from where you did not reckon; what carries no obligation to a creature and no liability upon you; what its earner does not disobey God in earning; what its earner does not forget God in. The goodly provision is licit in source and free in spirit.

mots.racine: ث م ر / ن خ ل / ع ن ب
ر ز ق / ح س ن

versets.ar: وَأَوْحَىٰ رَبُّكَ إِلَى النَّحْلِ أَنِ اتَّخِذِي مِنَ الْجِبَالِ بُيُوتًا وَمِنَ الشَّجَرِ وَمِمَّا يَعْرِشُونَ
versets.tr: wa-awḥā rabbuka ilā n-naḥli ani ttakhidhī mina l-jibāli buyūtan wa-mina sh-shajari wa-mimmā yaʿrishūna
versets.trad: And your Lord inspired the bee: “Take dwellings in the mountains, in the trees, and in what they build,
versets.commentaire: Here the sūra names the bee. Qushayrī: “He inspired the bee” — He meant an inspiration of suggestion (ilhām); and when it kept the command and ate of the lawful, its food was made good, and what comes from it was made a healing for people. He draws the great lesson: God made known to creation that preferment is not by analogy or merit, for the bee has no distinction in stature, form, or adornment — and yet from it He made honey, a healing for people. Man, with the perfection of his form and the fullness of his intellect, and the distinctions singled out for prophets and saints, has within him a desolation that is no secret. So what merit belongs to the bee, and what fault to man? It is nothing but His choosing.

mots.racine: و ح ي / ن ح ل
أ خ ذ / ج ب ل / ب ي ت

versets.ar: ثُمَّ كُلِي مِن كُلِّ الثَّمَرَاتِ فَاسْلُكِي سُبُلَ رَبِّكِ ذُلُلًا ۚ يَخْرُجُ مِن بُطُونِهَا شَرَابٌ مُّخْتَلِفٌ أَلْوَانُهُ فِيهِ شِفَاءٌ لِّلنَّاسِ ۗ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَآيَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: thumma kulī min kulli th-thamarāti fa-slukī subula rabbiki dhululan yakhruju min buṭūnihā sharābun mukhtalifun alwānuhu fīhi shifāʾun li-n-nāsi inna fī dhālika la-āyatan li-qawmin yatafakkarūna
versets.trad: then eat of all the fruits, and follow humbly the ways of your Lord.” From their bellies comes a drink of varied colors in which there is healing for people. In that is truly a sign for a people who reflect.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī extends the bee's secret into a law of grace: God has run His way that He hides every precious thing within something lowly — He placed silk in the worm, the weakest of creatures; honey in the bee, the weakest of flying things; the pearl in the shell, the most desolate of sea-creatures; and gold, silver, and turquoise in stone. Likewise He deposited His gnosis and His love in the hearts of the believers — and among them are those who disobey and those who err.

mots.racine: أ ك ل / ث م ر
س ل ك / س ب ل / ذ ل ل
ش ف ي / ن و س

versets.ar: وَاللَّهُ خَلَقَكُمْ ثُمَّ يَتَوَفَّاكُمْ ۚ وَمِنكُم مَّن يُرَدُّ إِلَىٰ أَرْذَلِ الْعُمُرِ لِكَيْ لَا يَعْلَمَ بَعْدَ عِلْمٍ شَيْئًا ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-llāhu khalaqakum thumma yatawaffākum wa-minkum man yuraddu ilā ardhali l-ʿumuri li-kay lā yaʿlama baʿda ʿilmin shayʾan inna llāha ʿalīmun qadīrun
versets.trad: God created you, then takes you in death; and among you is one who is brought back to the most abject age, so that after having known, he knows nothing. Indeed God is all-knowing, all-powerful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī enumerates meanings of “the most abject age”: that one be returned to forsaking after enabling — obedient at the start of his life, then disobedient at its end; or that he be drawn, in the prime of youth, into aspiration and walk the path of God for a time, then a lapse befalls him, he unties the knot of his resolve and returns to seeking the world — and among the folk this is an apostasy on the Path; or that it be the elder's craving in seeking, or a man's love of leadership.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / و ف ي
mots.sens: created you then takes you in death

mots.racine: ر ذ ل / ع م ر
ع ل م / ق د ر

versets.ar: وَاللَّهُ فَضَّلَ بَعْضَكُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ فِي الرِّزْقِ ۚ فَمَا الَّذِينَ فُضِّلُوا بِرَادِّي رِزْقِهِمْ عَلَىٰ مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُهُمْ فَهُمْ فِيهِ سَوَاءٌ ۚ أَفَبِنِعْمَةِ اللَّهِ يَجْحَدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-llāhu faḍḍala baʿḍakum ʿalā baʿḍin fī r-rizqi fa-mā lladhīna fuḍḍilū bi-rāddī rizqihim ʿalā mā malakat aymānuhum fa-hum fīhi sawāʾun a-fa-bi-niʿmati llāhi yajḥadūna
versets.trad: God has favored some of you over others in provision. Those favored will not hand over their provision to those their right hands possess, that they should be equal in it. Will they then deny the favor of God?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the kinds of provision: there is provision for the soul, for the heart, for the spirit, for the inmost secret. The soul's provision comes to some through the grace of obedience, to others through abandonment to sins; the heart's, to some through the presence sustained by reflection, to others through the dominion of heedlessness; the spirit's, to some through the purity of love; and the provision of the secrets is received only in the contemplation of the Real. Whoever is not of this is not among the people of the secret.

versets.ar: وَاللَّهُ جَعَلَ لَكُم مِّنْ أَنفُسِكُمْ أَزْوَاجًا وَجَعَلَ لَكُم مِّنْ أَزْوَاجِكُم بَنِينَ وَحَفَدَةً وَرَزَقَكُم مِّنَ الطَّيِّبَاتِ ۚ أَفَبِالْبَاطِلِ يُؤْمِنُونَ وَبِنِعْمَتِ اللَّهِ هُمْ يَكْفُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-llāhu jaʿala lakum min anfusikum azwājan wa-jaʿala lakum min azwājikum banīna wa-ḥafadatan wa-razaqakum mina ṭ-ṭayyibāti a-fa-bi-l-bāṭili yuʾminūna wa-bi-niʿmati llāhi hum yakfurūna
versets.trad: God made for you spouses from among yourselves, and gave you from your spouses children and grandchildren, and provided you with good things. Will they then believe in falsehood and deny the favor of God?
versets.commentaire: Willing the continuance of the species, the Real arranged the causes of union and procreation. For the “good things” (ṭayyibāt) Qushayrī distinguishes: one savors a food or a drink, another savors seclusion and purity. And he glosses the “falsehood” they believe in: it is the reckoning that some help comes from other-than-God and the attaching of the heart to them; and the favor they deny is trust in God, the awaiting of relief from Him, and the beauty of reliance upon Him.

mots.racine: ب ن ي / ح ف د

versets.ar: وَيَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ مَا لَا يَمْلِكُ لَهُمْ رِزْقًا مِّنَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ شَيْئًا وَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yaʿbudūna min dūni llāhi mā lā yamliku lahum rizqan mina s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi shayʾan wa-lā yastaṭīʿūna
versets.trad: They worship besides God that which has no power to provide them anything from the heavens or the earth, and is incapable.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī widens the reproach beyond idolaters: whoever attaches himself to a person or a means resembles the worshippers of idols, in that he squanders his time in what does not avail him; for provision, in truth, is apportioned by God alone. To treat means as if they held power is to forget that all power belongs to the Real.

versets.ar: فَلَا تَضْرِبُوا لِلَّهِ الْأَمْثَالَ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ وَأَنتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lā taḍribū li-llāhi l-amthāla inna llāha yaʿlamu wa-antum lā taʿlamūna
versets.trad: So do not strike likenesses for God. God knows, and you do not know.
versets.commentaire: How can likenesses be struck for Him whom none equals in essence, attributes, or the rulings of His acts? Qushayrī warns: whoever regards the Real through the created falls into the darkness of assimilation (tashbīh) and remains cut off from the knowledge of the Worshipped. Every measure drawn from creation is a veil; transcendence refuses the mirror of creatures.

versets.ar: ضَرَبَ اللَّهُ مَثَلًا عَبْدًا مَّمْلُوكًا لَّا يَقْدِرُ عَلَىٰ شَيْءٍ وَمَن رَّزَقْنَاهُ مِنَّا رِزْقًا حَسَنًا فَهُوَ يُنفِقُ مِنْهُ سِرًّا وَجَهْرًا ۚ هَلْ يَسْتَوُونَ ۚ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ۚ بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: ḍaraba llāhu mathalan ʿabdan mamlūkan lā yaqdiru ʿalā shayʾin wa-man razaqnāhu minnā rizqan ḥasanan fa-huwa yunfiqu minhu sirran wa-jahran hal yastawūna l-ḥamdu li-llāhi bal aktharuhum lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: God strikes a parable: a slave, owned, who has no power over anything, and one whom We have provided from Us with goodly provision, from which he spends secretly and openly. Are they equal? Praise be to God! But most of them do not know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads the parable as the contrast of disbeliever and believer: the disbeliever is like the owned slave who has power over nothing; the sincere believer like one whom God has lavished with good things and enabled to obedience, then promised reward and a fair return. And he carries it further: he who dwells absorbed in himself, mindful of his peers, prisoner of his illusions, is not like him who perceives by his Lord, snatched from his own witnessing, absent from all else, moved by his Lord with no power but through Him.

mots.racine: ع ب د / م ل ك
ن ف ق / س ر ر / ج ه ر

versets.ar: وَضَرَبَ اللَّهُ مَثَلًا رَّجُلَيْنِ أَحَدُهُمَا أَبْكَمُ لَا يَقْدِرُ عَلَىٰ شَيْءٍ وَهُوَ كَلٌّ عَلَىٰ مَوْلَاهُ أَيْنَمَا يُوَجِّههُّ لَا يَأْتِ بِخَيْرٍ ۖ هَلْ يَسْتَوِي هُوَ وَمَن يَأْمُرُ بِالْعَدْلِ ۙ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ صِرَاطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ
versets.tr: wa-ḍaraba llāhu mathalan rajulayni aḥaduhumā abkamu lā yaqdiru ʿalā shayʾin wa-huwa kallun ʿalā mawlāhu aynamā yuwajjihhu lā yaʾti bi-khayrin hal yastawī huwa wa-man yaʾmuru bi-l-ʿadli wa-huwa ʿalā ṣirāṭin mustaqīmin
versets.trad: And God strikes a parable of two men: one is mute, with no power over anything, a burden to his master; wherever he sends him, he brings no good. Is he equal to one who enjoins justice and is on a straight path?
versets.commentaire: This second parable too holds for believer and disbeliever. Qushayrī: the disbeliever is like the mute ignoramus from whom nothing issues and no benefit comes; the believer is on the straight path, disavowing his own power and strength, acknowledging only the bounty and grace of his Lord. The justice that is enjoined springs from this self-effacement in which the servant claims nothing.

mots.racine: ك ل ل / و ل ي
أ م ر / ع د ل

versets.ar: وَلِلَّهِ غَيْبُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۚ وَمَا أَمْرُ السَّاعَةِ إِلَّا كَلَمْحِ الْبَصَرِ أَوْ هُوَ أَقْرَبُ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: wa-li-llāhi ghaybu s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-mā amru s-sāʿati illā ka-lamḥi l-baṣari aw huwa aqrabu inna llāha ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīrun
versets.trad: To God belongs the unseen of the heavens and the earth. The matter of the Hour is but as a glance of the eye, or nearer still. Indeed God is powerful over all things.
versets.commentaire: The Real reserved to Himself the knowledge of the unseen and veiled it from creatures. Qushayrī: He brings forth some in misguidance then transports them to the state of friendship (wilāya); He marks others with enmity then returns them to friendship. The ends are veiled, the final outcomes obscure, and creatures heedless of what is intended for them. The eye-glance of the Hour speaks His absolute sway over time.

mots.racine: غ ي ب / س م و
ل م ح / ب ص ر
mots.sens: as a glance of the eye

versets.ar: وَاللَّهُ أَخْرَجَكُم مِّن بُطُونِ أُمَّهَاتِكُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ شَيْئًا وَجَعَلَ لَكُمُ السَّمْعَ وَالْأَبْصَارَ وَالْأَفْئِدَةَ ۙ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-llāhu akhrajakum min buṭūni ummahātikum lā taʿlamūna shayʾan wa-jaʿala lakumu s-samʿa wa-l-abṣāra wa-l-afʾidata laʿallakum tashkurūna
versets.trad: God brought you out of your mothers' wombs knowing nothing, and gave you hearing, sight, and hearts, that perhaps you may be grateful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He created them without consulting them, established them in the state He willed without giving them choice; they knew nothing of what had preceded their decree — were they created for felicity or drawn from nothingness to wretchedness? Then by the way of inspiration He guided them, until the infant took its mother's breast with no prior teaching. And he glosses the gifts: hearing, that you may hear His address; sight, that you may behold His acts; the heart, that you may know His right — then give thanks.

mots.racine: س م ع / ب ص ر

versets.ar: أَلَمْ يَرَوْا إِلَى الطَّيْرِ مُسَخَّرَاتٍ فِي جَوِّ السَّمَاءِ مَا يُمْسِكُهُنَّ إِلَّا اللَّهُ ۗ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَآيَاتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: a-lam yaraw ilā ṭ-ṭayri musakhkharātin fī jawwi s-samāʾi mā yumsikuhunna illā llāhu inna fī dhālika la-āyātin li-qawmin yuʾminūna
versets.trad: Do they not see the birds, subjected in the air of the sky, held aloft by none but God? In that are truly signs for a people who believe.
versets.commentaire: The bird that soars in the air remains as if suspended without falling. Qushayrī sees in it the clear proof that the Real alone gives existence: no event escapes His power, and the bird's poise in the void attests the perfection of His might. What appears as natural law is the invisible Hand that holds.

versets.ar: وَاللَّهُ جَعَلَ لَكُم مِّن بُيُوتِكُمْ سَكَنًا وَجَعَلَ لَكُم مِّن جُلُودِ الْأَنْعَامِ بُيُوتًا تَسْتَخِفُّونَهَا يَوْمَ ظَعْنِكُمْ وَيَوْمَ إِقَامَتِكُمْ ۙ وَمِنْ أَصْوَافِهَا وَأَوْبَارِهَا وَأَشْعَارِهَا أَثَاثًا وَمَتَاعًا إِلَىٰ حِينٍ
versets.tr: wa-llāhu jaʿala lakum min buyūtikum sakanan wa-jaʿala lakum min julūdi l-anʿāmi buyūtan tastakhiffūnahā yawma ẓaʿnikum wa-yawma iqāmatikum wa-min aṣwāfihā wa-awbārihā wa-ashʿārihā athāthan wa-matāʿan ilā ḥīnin
versets.trad: God made for you, in your houses, repose, and made for you, from the hides of cattle, dwellings light for you on the day of your travel and the day of your encampment; and from their wool, fur, and hair, furnishings and enjoyment for a time.
versets.commentaire: To the rest of houses Qushayrī answers an inner rest: there is a homeland for souls and a homeland for hearts. People are of two kinds: settled and traveling. The novice (murīd) is a traveler by his heart, for he changes and rises from rank to rank; the knower (ʿārif) is settled and established, for he has arrived and is firm. The stations of the Path are crossed not with souls but with hearts: the novice journeys, the knower has arrived.

mots.racine: ج ل د / ن ع م
أ ث ث / م ت ع

versets.ar: وَاللَّهُ جَعَلَ لَكُم مِّمَّا خَلَقَ ظِلَالًا وَجَعَلَ لَكُم مِّنَ الْجِبَالِ أَكْنَانًا وَجَعَلَ لَكُمْ سَرَابِيلَ تَقِيكُمُ الْحَرَّ وَسَرَابِيلَ تَقِيكُم بَأْسَكُمْ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُتِمُّ نِعْمَتَهُ عَلَيْكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُسْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-llāhu jaʿala lakum mimmā khalaqa ẓilālan wa-jaʿala lakum mina l-jibāli aknānan wa-jaʿala lakum sarābīla taqīkumu l-ḥarra wa-sarābīla taqīkum baʾsakum ka-dhālika yutimmu niʿmatahu ʿalaykum laʿallakum tuslimūna
versets.trad: God has made for you shade from what He created, and made for you refuges in the mountains, and made for you garments that protect you from heat and garments that protect you in your fighting. Thus He completes His favor upon you, that perhaps you may submit.
versets.commentaire: To the outward shade Qushayrī answers the shade of solicitude: just as He covered your outward selves with tunics that guard you from heat and from blows, He clothed your secrets with a garment that wraps you in ease and trial; the garment of preservation (ʿiṣma) guards you from disobeying Him, the shade of enabling carries you to abide in His worship, and the robes of union qualify you for His nearness. The completion of favor is that the end be sealed with good.

versets.ar: فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا فَإِنَّمَا عَلَيْكَ الْبَلَاغُ الْمُبِينُ
versets.tr: fa-in tawallaw fa-innamā ʿalayka l-balāghu l-mubīnu
versets.trad: But if they turn away, upon you is only the clear delivery.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses: once the message is delivered, We have not placed in your hands the decree of guidance and misguidance. To the Messenger belongs the clear communication; to God alone to guide hearts or veil them. The servant discharges his task and effaces himself before the sovereignty.

versets.ar: يَعْرِفُونَ نِعْمَتَ اللَّهِ ثُمَّ يُنكِرُونَهَا وَأَكْثَرُهُمُ الْكَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: yaʿrifūna niʿmata llāhi thumma yunkirūnahā wa-aktharuhumu l-kāfirūna
versets.trad: They recognize the favor of God, then they deny it. Most of them are disbelievers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī multiplies the figures of this aborted recognition: they are enabled toward obedience, then grow proud of it; they cry out, then, once answered, fall short in thanks; an affliction strikes them, they take refuge in their Lord, and once delivered they forget their distress and return to the evil that caused it; in repentance they know the ugliness of their slip, then, breaking their repentance, it is as if they had never known that state.

versets.ar: وَيَوْمَ نَبْعَثُ مِن كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ شَهِيدًا ثُمَّ لَا يُؤْذَنُ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَلَا هُمْ يُسْتَعْتَبُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yawma nabʿathu min kulli ummatin shahīdan thumma lā yuʾdhanu li-lladhīna kafarū wa-lā hum yustaʿtabūna
versets.trad: On the Day We raise from every community a witness, no leave will be given to the disbelievers, nor will they be allowed to make amends.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: on the Day of gathering the messengers will be questioned about the states of their communities; whoever speaks with a proof is honored, and whoever advances no argument will see no sanctuary granted him. The hour of excuse is past; only the bare reckoning of what one has borne remains.

mots.def: istaʿtaba: to seek to regain favor.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا رَأَى الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا الْعَذَابَ فَلَا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُمْ وَلَا هُمْ يُنظَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā raʾā lladhīna ẓalamū l-ʿadhāba fa-lā yukhaffafu ʿanhum wa-lā hum yunẓarūna
versets.trad: When the wrongdoers see the punishment, it will not be lightened for them, nor will they be granted respite.
versets.commentaire: Brief and decisive, Qushayrī glosses: the affair will be made severe upon them and not eased. The punishment closes without reprieve or delay for the wrongdoers, sealing the prolonged refusal of an entire life.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا رَأَى الَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا شُرَكَاءَهُمْ قَالُوا رَبَّنَا هَٰؤُلَاءِ شُرَكَاؤُنَا الَّذِينَ كُنَّا نَدْعُو مِن دُونِكَ ۖ فَأَلْقَوْا إِلَيْهِمُ الْقَوْلَ إِنَّكُمْ لَكَاذِبُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā raʾā lladhīna ashrakū shurakāʾahum qālū rabbanā hāʾulāʾi shurakāʾunā lladhīna kunnā nadʿū min dūnika fa-alqaw ilayhimu l-qawla innakum la-kādhibūna
versets.trad: When the associators see those they associated, they will say, “Our Lord, these are our associates whom we invoked besides You.” But they will retort to them, “You are surely liars!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī paints the rupture of complicities: they will wish to avenge themselves on those who kept their company and drove them to the slip; so they will disown their associates, curse one another, and their breasts will be straitened against one another. The alliance built on misguidance turns to hatred on the Day of unveiling.

versets.ar: وَأَلْقَوْا إِلَى اللَّهِ يَوْمَئِذٍ السَّلَمَ ۖ وَضَلَّ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-alqaw ilā llāhi yawmaʾidhin as-salama wa-ḍalla ʿanhum mā kānū yaftarūna
versets.trad: On that day they will offer submission to God, and what they used to invent will forsake them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they will surrender to God's command and judgment; on that day no plea of theirs will be seen, and no trial whose woe they cry out from will be lifted off them. The fictions they had forged vanish, and nothing remains but surrender without recourse.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / س ل م

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَصَدُّوا عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ زِدْنَاهُمْ عَذَابًا فَوْقَ الْعَذَابِ بِمَا كَانُوا يُفْسِدُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna kafarū wa-ṣaddū ʿan sabīli llāhi zidnāhum ʿadhāban fawqa l-ʿadhābi bi-mā kānū yufsidūna
versets.trad: Those who disbelieved and barred others from the way of God — We will add for them punishment upon punishment for the corruption they spread.
versets.commentaire: The disbeliever who also turns others from the Path amasses two strayings: his own denial and the corruption he spreads.
