Qushayrī recalls that punishment is measured by the deed: for the simple fault, one penalty; for the fault that contaminates, a penalty above the penalty, the just return of the evil sown in others.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ف و ق

versets.ar: وَيَوْمَ نَبْعَثُ فِي كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ شَهِيدًا عَلَيْهِم مِّنْ أَنفُسِهِمْ ۖ وَجِئْنَا بِكَ شَهِيدًا عَلَىٰ هَٰؤُلَاءِ ۚ وَنَزَّلْنَا عَلَيْكَ الْكِتَابَ تِبْيَانًا لِّكُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةً وَبُشْرَىٰ لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-yawma nabʿathu fī kulli ummatin shahīdan ʿalayhim min anfusihim wa-jiʾnā bika shahīdan ʿalā hāʾulāʾi wa-nazzalnā ʿalayka l-kitāba tibyānan li-kulli shayʾin wa-hudan wa-raḥmatan wa-bushrā li-l-muslimīna
versets.trad: On the Day We raise in every community a witness against them from among themselves, and bring you as a witness against these — We have sent down to you the Book as a clarification of all things, and as guidance, mercy, and good news for those who submit.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī exalts the rank of the community and its Prophet: on the Day of Resurrection each community will come with its messenger — but no community equals this one in merit, no messenger equals ours in rank and dignity. And of the Book he says: a clarification of all things; for the believers a healing and a light, and for the deniers a trial, the cause of their affliction.

mots.racine: ب ش ر / س ل م
mots.sens: good news for those who submit

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَأْمُرُ بِالْعَدْلِ وَالْإِحْسَانِ وَإِيتَاءِ ذِي الْقُرْبَىٰ وَيَنْهَىٰ عَنِ الْفَحْشَاءِ وَالْمُنكَرِ وَالْبَغْيِ ۚ يَعِظُكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: inna llāha yaʾmuru bi-l-ʿadli wa-l-iḥsāni wa-ītāʾi dhī l-qurbā wa-yanhā ʿani l-faḥshāʾi wa-l-munkari wa-l-baghyi yaʿiẓukum laʿallakum tadhakkarūna
versets.trad: Indeed God enjoins justice, excellence (iḥsān), and giving to kinsfolk, and forbids indecency, wrongdoing, and oppression. He admonishes you, that perhaps you may take heed.
versets.commentaire: A major verse. Qushayrī unfolds justice on three planes: justice between man and his soul, restraining it from what would ruin it — its perfection is to cauterize the veins of greed; justice between man and his Lord, preferring His right over the soul's lot and his Master's pleasure over all else; justice between man and creatures, through sincere counsel and the abandonment of all treachery. As for iḥsān, he recalls the ḥadīth: “that you worship God as if you saw Him” — and that is the state of contemplation the folk point to.

mots.racine: ع د ل / ح س ن
mots.def: ʿadl: justice; iḥsān: doing good, excellence.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ق ر ب
ف ح ش / ن ك ر

versets.ar: وَأَوْفُوا بِعَهْدِ اللَّهِ إِذَا عَاهَدتُّمْ وَلَا تَنقُضُوا الْأَيْمَانَ بَعْدَ تَوْكِيدِهَا وَقَدْ جَعَلْتُمُ اللَّهَ عَلَيْكُمْ كَفِيلًا ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا تَفْعَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-awfū bi-ʿahdi llāhi idhā ʿāhadtum wa-lā tanquḍū l-aymāna baʿda tawkīdihā wa-qad jaʿaltumu llāha ʿalaykum kafīlan inna llāha yaʿlamu mā tafʿalūna
versets.trad: Fulfill the covenant of God when you have made it, and do not break the oaths after their confirmation, when you have made God a surety over you. Indeed God knows what you do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the covenant a stairway of fidelities proper to each rank: the ascetic has covenanted not to return to the world — if he returns, he has broken it; the worshipper has covenanted to forsake passion; the novice, to leave habit; the knower, to strip himself for Him and disown all else; the lover, to give up his very self in everything; and the unifier, to efface himself in Him and single Him out by every path. To each his oath, and to all the duty to keep it.

mots.racine: و ف ي / ع ه د
ن ق ض / ي م ن

versets.ar: وَلَا تَكُونُوا كَالَّتِي نَقَضَتْ غَزْلَهَا مِن بَعْدِ قُوَّةٍ أَنكَاثًا تَتَّخِذُونَ أَيْمَانَكُمْ دَخَلًا بَيْنَكُمْ أَن تَكُونَ أُمَّةٌ هِيَ أَرْبَىٰ مِنْ أُمَّةٍ ۚ إِنَّمَا يَبْلُوكُمُ اللَّهُ بِهِ ۚ وَلَيُبَيِّنَنَّ لَكُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ مَا كُنتُمْ فِيهِ تَخْتَلِفُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā takūnū ka-llatī naqaḍat ghazlahā min baʿdi quwwatin ankāthan tattakhidhūna aymānakum dakhalan baynakum an takūna ummatun hiya arbā min ummatin innamā yablūkumu llāhu bihi wa-la-yubayyinanna lakum yawma l-qiyāmati mā kuntum fīhi takhtalifūna
versets.trad: Do not be like she who unraveled her yarn after it was firmly spun, taking your oaths as a means of deceit among yourselves, so that one community might gain over another. God only tests you by it, and on the Day of Resurrection He will make clear to you that wherein you differed.
versets.commentaire: Whoever breaks his covenant ruins by his end his beginning and tears from his own hand what he had planted. Qushayrī makes it the image of spiritual lapses: when the wayfarer is overtaken by langor, the novice by a halt on the Path, the knower by a veiling, the lover by a separation — these are immense trials and rending afflictions. For when the Real wills to test a people, their hearts and their gazes are overturned. And he distinguishes the trial of the common, which falls on goods, from the trial of the elect, which rends the heart of love.

mots.racine: ن ق ض / غ ز ل
mots.def: ghazl: spun thread; naqaḍa: to undo.

versets.ar: وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ لَجَعَلَكُمْ أُمَّةً وَاحِدَةً وَلَٰكِن يُضِلُّ مَن يَشَاءُ وَيَهْدِي مَن يَشَاءُ ۚ وَلَتُسْأَلُنَّ عَمَّا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-law shāʾa llāhu la-jaʿalakum ummatan wāḥidatan wa-lākin yuḍillu man yashāʾu wa-yahdī man yashāʾu wa-la-tusʾalunna ʿammā kuntum taʿmalūna
versets.trad: Had God willed, He would have made you a single community. But He lets stray whom He wills and guides whom He wills. And you will surely be questioned about what you used to do.
versets.commentaire: The affair of humankind, says Qushayrī, does not rest only on their losses or shortcomings: these are apparent causes, but the secret of the matter is elsewhere. And he glosses: had God willed their felicity, He would have shown them mercy, preserved them from sins, and inspired them with constant remembrance in place of heedlessness — but the apportionment preceded. Before this mystery the servant bows and sings his helplessness.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / و ح د
mots.sens: He would have made you one community

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ش ي أ
mots.sens: He lets stray whom He wills

versets.ar: وَلَا تَتَّخِذُوا أَيْمَانَكُمْ دَخَلًا بَيْنَكُمْ فَتَزِلَّ قَدَمٌ بَعْدَ ثُبُوتِهَا وَتَذُوقُوا السُّوءَ بِمَا صَدَدتُّمْ عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ۖ وَلَكُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lā tattakhidhū aymānakum dakhalan baynakum fa-tazilla qadamun baʿda thubūtihā wa-tadhūqū s-sūʾa bi-mā ṣadadtum ʿan sabīli llāhi wa-lakum ʿadhābun ʿaẓīmun
versets.trad: Do not take your oaths as a means of deceit among yourselves, lest a foot should slip after its firmness, and you taste evil for having barred others from the way of God; and yours would be a great punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the slipping foot a parable of doubt: the lack of sincerity in your faith has kept you from realizing your proof, for you halted at the frontier of hesitation without deciding or fixing; and your hesitation led you to the homelands of association — for doubt in God and association with Him are twins in the ruling. The betrayed oath is the first slip.

mots.sens: a means of deceit among you

mots.racine: ز ل ل / ق د م

versets.ar: وَلَا تَشْتَرُوا بِعَهْدِ اللَّهِ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا ۚ إِنَّمَا عِندَ اللَّهِ هُوَ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā tashtarū bi-ʿahdi llāhi thamanan qalīlan innamā ʿinda llāhi huwa khayrun lakum in kuntum taʿlamūna
versets.trad: Do not sell the covenant of God for a paltry price. What is with God is better for you, if only you knew!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī exhorts: do not prefer, at the price of fulfilling God's right and being faithful to His covenant, a meager exchange from the refuse of your world, lawful or unlawful; for what God has prepared for you in His gardens — on condition of your faithfulness to your faith — fulfills and surpasses the lots you hasten to seize here. The covenant sold for little is the maddest of bargains.

mots.racine: ث م ن / ق ل ل

versets.ar: مَا عِندَكُمْ يَنفَدُ ۖ وَمَا عِندَ اللَّهِ بَاقٍ ۗ وَلَنَجْزِيَنَّ الَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا أَجْرَهُم بِأَحْسَنِ مَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: mā ʿindakum yanfadu wa-mā ʿinda llāhi bāqin wa-la-najziyanna lladhīna ṣabarū ajrahum bi-aḥsani mā kānū yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: What is with you runs out, and what is with God endures. We shall surely reward those who were patient according to the best of what they used to do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opposes the transient to the abiding even in love: what is of you — your yearning toward Our meeting — is exposed to extinction and subject to ending; but what We have qualified Ourselves with, the welcome turned toward you, knows no term, a bounty that does not run dry. And he cites the holy word: “The longing of the righteous for My meeting has grown long, and I, toward meeting them, am of yet more ardent longing.”

mots.racine: ج ز ي / ص ب ر
mots.def: jazā: to reward; ṣabara: to be patient.

versets.ar: مَنْ عَمِلَ صَالِحًا مِّن ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنثَىٰ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَلَنُحْيِيَنَّهُ حَيَاةً طَيِّبَةً ۖ وَلَنَجْزِيَنَّهُمْ أَجْرَهُم بِأَحْسَنِ مَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: man ʿamila ṣāliḥan min dhakarin aw unthā wa-huwa muʾminun fa-la-nuḥyiyannahu ḥayātan ṭayyibatan wa-la-najziyannahum ajrahum bi-aḥsani mā kānū yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: Whoever does good, male or female, while being a believer — We shall surely give them a good life, and We shall reward them according to the best of what they used to do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī dwells on the “good life” (ḥayāt ṭayyiba), which none can name but which is known by taste: some have called it the sweetness of obedience, some contentment, some acceptance, some intimate converse (najwā), some the breeze of nearness — and all are true, each according to his portion. And he gives its summit: the good life is to be with the Beloved, where joy is completed only by His presence.

mots.racine: ع م ل / ص ل ح
ح ي ي / ط ي ب

versets.ar: فَإِذَا قَرَأْتَ الْقُرْآنَ فَاسْتَعِذْ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيمِ
versets.tr: fa-idhā qaraʾta l-qurʾāna fa-staʿidh bi-llāhi mina sh-shayṭāni r-rajīmi
versets.trad: When you recite the Qurʾān, seek refuge in God from Satan the accursed.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī interiorizes the devil: each person's Satan is whatever distracts him from his Lord. Whoever is dominated by his soul until it distracts him from his Lord — even by the witnessing of an act of obedience, the relishing of worship, or the regard of a state — that is his Satan. The duty then is to seek refuge in God from the evil of one's own soul and of every bearer of evil.

mots.racine: ش ط ن / ر ج م

versets.ar: إِنَّهُ لَيْسَ لَهُ سُلْطَانٌ عَلَى الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ
versets.tr: innahu laysa lahu sulṭānun ʿalā lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿalā rabbihim yatawakkalūna
versets.trad: He has no power over those who believe and put their trust in their Lord.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī almost marvels: how should Satan have power over the servant, when the Real alone creates and singly brings into being? He whose heart sees that all comes about by God offers no foothold to the enemy; trust (tawakkul) is the armor against all dominion.

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا سُلْطَانُهُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ يَتَوَلَّوْنَهُ وَالَّذِينَ هُم بِهِ مُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: innamā sulṭānuhu ʿalā lladhīna yatawallawnahu wa-lladhīna hum bihi mushrikūna
versets.trad: His power is only over those who take him as patron and who, through him, are associators.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: his dominion bears only on those who remain in the veil of their heedlessness, prisoners of their conjectures and ambiguities. As for the people of unity, they see things come about by God, begin from God, and to God return and end. The tawḥīdic gaze strips the adversary of all power.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا بَدَّلْنَا آيَةً مَّكَانَ آيَةٍ ۙ وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يُنَزِّلُ قَالُوا إِنَّمَا أَنتَ مُفْتَرٍ ۚ بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā baddalnā āyatan makāna āyatin wa-llāhu aʿlamu bi-mā yunazzilu qālū innamā anta muftarin bal aktharuhum lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: When We replace one verse with another — and God knows best what He sends down — they say, “You are only a forger!” Rather, most of them do not know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī observes that the length of time only thickened their denial: throughout their respite they increased only in doubt upon doubt and denial upon denial; they followed their path of disbelief and would not believe him, adding toward his cause only suspicion. The charge of forgery rebounds upon them: it is blindness, not the message, that is at fault.

mots.racine: ب د ل / أ ي ي
mots.def: baddala: to substitute; āya: sign, verse.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ن ز ل
mots.sens: knows best what He sends down

versets.ar: قُلْ نَزَّلَهُ رُوحُ الْقُدُسِ مِن رَّبِّكَ بِالْحَقِّ لِيُثَبِّتَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَهُدًى وَبُشْرَىٰ لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ
versets.tr: qul nazzalahu rūḥu l-qudusi min rabbika bi-l-ḥaqqi li-yuthabbita lladhīna āmanū wa-hudan wa-bushrā li-l-muslimīna
versets.trad: Say: “The Holy Spirit has brought it down from your Lord in truth, to make firm those who believe, and as guidance and good news for those who submit.”
versets.commentaire: The “Holy Spirit” (rūḥ al-qudus) is Gabriel, the angel of revelation. Qushayrī sees in the verse an answer to their ignorance: because they were scattered in the contemplation of the created kingdom, they were brought back, at the moment of instruction, by the mention of the angel. The descent “in truth” makes firm the believers, who receive the Book as guidance and glad tidings.

mots.racine: ر و ح / ق د س

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ نَعْلَمُ أَنَّهُمْ يَقُولُونَ إِنَّمَا يُعَلِّمُهُ بَشَرٌ ۗ لِّسَانُ الَّذِي يُلْحِدُونَ إِلَيْهِ أَعْجَمِيٌّ وَهَٰذَا لِسَانٌ عَرَبِيٌّ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: wa-la-qad naʿlamu annahum yaqūlūna innamā yuʿallimuhu basharun lisānu lladhī yulḥidūna ilayhi aʿjamiyyun wa-hādhā lisānun ʿarabiyyun mubīnun
versets.trad: We know well that they say, “It is only a mortal who teaches him.” But the tongue of the one they perversely allude to is foreign, while this is a clear Arabic tongue.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Messenger was not troubled by their denial nor by the veil that hid his rank from their eyes. What harm reaches one who sits in the company of the King, if his state is hidden from the basest of the rabble? And he sets up the argument: a Qurʾān that all creation is powerless to match in eloquence cannot proceed from a man of foreign tongue.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ب ش ر
ل س ن / ع ر ب / ب ي ن

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ لَا يَهْدِيهِمُ اللَّهُ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna lā yuʾminūna bi-āyāti llāhi lā yahdīhimu llāhu wa-lahum ʿadhābun alīmun
versets.trad: Those who do not believe in the signs of God — God does not guide them, and theirs is a painful punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī decides by the pre-eternal decree: whoever's portion was sealed in wretchedness does not receive the mercy of the Real; and whoever God has not guided here to His gnosis, He does not guide there to His garden. The present denial prolongs a destiny and calls forth a painful end.

mots.racine: أ م ن / أ ي ي
mots.sens: do not believe in God's signs

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / أ ل م

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا يَفْتَرِي الْكَذِبَ الَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ ۖ وَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْكَاذِبُونَ
versets.tr: innamā yaftarī l-kadhiba lladhīna lā yuʾminūna bi-āyāti llāhi wa-ulāʾika humu l-kādhibūna
versets.trad: Only those who do not believe in the signs of God forge lies, and it is they who are the liars.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī admires the subtlety of the reversal: this is one of the fine turns; when they accused the Prophet of forgery, the Real illumined the answer, saying: you are not the forger — the forger is he who denies his Worshipped and is ignorant of His oneness. The accusation rebounds upon the accuser.

mots.racine: ف ر ي / ك ذ ب

versets.ar: مَن كَفَرَ بِاللَّهِ مِن بَعْدِ إِيمَانِهِ إِلَّا مَنْ أُكْرِهَ وَقَلْبُهُ مُطْمَئِنٌّ بِالْإِيمَانِ وَلَٰكِن مَّن شَرَحَ بِالْكُفْرِ صَدْرًا فَعَلَيْهِمْ غَضَبٌ مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: man kafara bi-llāhi min baʿdi īmānihi illā man ukriha wa-qalbuhu muṭmaʾinnun bi-l-īmāni wa-lākin man sharaḥa bi-l-kufri ṣadran fa-ʿalayhim ghaḍabun mina llāhi wa-lahum ʿadhābun ʿaẓīmun
versets.trad: Whoever disbelieves in God after his belief — save one who is compelled while his heart is at peace in faith — but whoever opens his breast to disbelief, upon them is wrath from God, and theirs is a great punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the mercy toward the firm heart: when God knows the sincerity of His servant in his heart and the purity of his resolve, and a compulsion seizes him, He lightens the ruling upon him and wards off the hardship: one who utters the word of disbelief only under coercion remains a unifier, excused between himself and God. Likewise, he says, those vowed to the Path whom causes or excuses turn aside for a moment do not thereby break their covenant. But whoever opens his breast to denial has broken it by his own choice.

mots.racine: ق ل ب / ط م ن / أ م ن
mots.sens: his heart at peace in faith

mots.racine: ش ر ح / ك ف ر / ص د ر
mots.def: sharaḥa ṣadran: to expand, take delight.

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمُ اسْتَحَبُّوا الْحَيَاةَ الدُّنْيَا عَلَى الْآخِرَةِ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: dhālika bi-annahumu staḥabbū l-ḥayāta d-dunyā ʿalā l-ākhirati wa-anna llāha lā yahdī l-qawma l-kāfirīna
versets.trad: That is because they preferred the life of this world over the hereafter, and because God does not guide the disbelieving people.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī applies the verse to the wayfarer: when the traveler prefers the lots of the soul over the rights of the Real, he remains cut off from God, and no blessing is granted him in what he preferred over God's right. Preferring the transient over the eternal is the root of deprivation.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / د ن و
ه د ي / ك ف ر

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ طَبَعَ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ وَسَمْعِهِمْ وَأَبْصَارِهِمْ ۖ وَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْغَافِلُونَ
versets.tr: ulāʾika lladhīna ṭabaʿa llāhu ʿalā qulūbihim wa-samʿihim wa-abṣārihim wa-ulāʾika humu l-ghāfilūna
versets.trad: They are the ones whose hearts, hearing, and sight God has sealed; and they are the heedless.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the hardening a slope: when one persists in heedlessness without retrieving his state by the diligence of regret, he adds hardness upon hardness and no longer profits from the strength he possessed. The seal is no sudden arbitrariness but the term of a sustained neglect.

mots.racine: ق ل ب / س م ع

versets.ar: لَا جَرَمَ أَنَّهُمْ فِي الْآخِرَةِ هُمُ الْخَاسِرُونَ
versets.tr: lā jarama annahum fī l-ākhirati humu l-khāsirūna
versets.trad: Without doubt, in the hereafter it is they who are the losers.
versets.commentaire: Briefly, Qushayrī: in the hereafter they are veiled, and marked with the abasement of distance. The supreme loss is not a damage of goods but exile far from the Face.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لِلَّذِينَ هَاجَرُوا مِن بَعْدِ مَا فُتِنُوا ثُمَّ جَاهَدُوا وَصَبَرُوا إِنَّ رَبَّكَ مِن بَعْدِهَا لَغَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: thumma inna rabbaka li-lladhīna hājarū min baʿdi mā futinū thumma jāhadū wa-ṣabarū inna rabbaka min baʿdihā la-ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: Then indeed your Lord, to those who emigrated after being persecuted, then strove and were patient — indeed your Lord, after that, is forgiving, merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī exalts the one who, when trial comes, does not bend toward ease: whoever is patient when the affair grows hard, inclines not to the side of concessions, and takes in all things the harder part — God honors his right, draws his place near, welcomes him in every state with an increase, and his trade prospers where his fellows are ruined. After the trial, the Lord turns to him as forgiveness and mercy.

mots.racine: ج ه د / ص ب ر
mots.def: jāhada: to strive; ṣabara: to endure.

versets.ar: يَوْمَ تَأْتِي كُلُّ نَفْسٍ تُجَادِلُ عَن نَّفْسِهَا وَتُوَفَّىٰ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَّا عَمِلَتْ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: yawma taʾtī kullu nafsin tujādilu ʿan nafsihā wa-tuwaffā kullu nafsin mā ʿamilat wa-hum lā yuẓlamūna
versets.trad: The Day when every soul comes pleading for itself, and every soul is repaid in full for what it did, and they are not wronged.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws a striking spiritual point: tomorrow each will be occupied with himself, with no leisure for another; yet how rare the servant who is not occupied with himself. None will be free tomorrow but he who is free today, and none will plead for his soul but he who is anxious for it today. Now the believer has no soul of his own — for God, he says, has bought from the believers their souls: He deposited them in their keeping; they have no claim of their own in them and tend them only by His command.

mots.racine: ج د ل / ن ف س

versets.ar: وَضَرَبَ اللَّهُ مَثَلًا قَرْيَةً كَانَتْ آمِنَةً مُّطْمَئِنَّةً يَأْتِيهَا رِزْقُهَا رَغَدًا مِّن كُلِّ مَكَانٍ فَكَفَرَتْ بِأَنْعُمِ اللَّهِ فَأَذَاقَهَا اللَّهُ لِبَاسَ الْجُوعِ وَالْخَوْفِ بِمَا كَانُوا يَصْنَعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ḍaraba llāhu mathalan qaryatan kānat āminatan muṭmaʾinnatan yaʾtīhā rizquhā raghadan min kulli makānin fa-kafarat bi-anʿumi llāhi fa-adhāqahā llāhu libāsa l-jūʿi wa-l-khawfi bi-mā kānū yaṣnaʿūna
versets.trad: God strikes a parable of a town that was secure and at peace, its provision coming to it in plenty from every place; then it denied the favors of God, so God made it taste the garment of hunger and fear for what they used to do.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī interiorizes the town: the heart's freedom from all encumbrance is an immense blessing; if the servant denies this blessing by opening to his soul the door of passion and being swept into the corruption of desire, God troubles his heart and strips him of the purity of moment he tasted, for the assaults of the soul bring on the eclipse of the heart's gleams. The peaceful town turned hungry figures the soul that betrays its quiet.

mots.racine: ق ر ي / أ م ن
ر ز ق / ر غ د
ل ب س / ج و ع / خ و ف
mots.sens: the garment of hunger and fear
mots.def: libās: garment; jūʿ: hunger; khawf: fear.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ جَاءَهُمْ رَسُولٌ مِّنْهُمْ فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَخَذَهُمُ الْعَذَابُ وَهُمْ ظَالِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-la-qad jāʾahum rasūlun minhum fa-kadhdhabūhu fa-akhadhahumu l-ʿadhābu wa-hum ẓālimūna
versets.trad: A messenger from among them came to them, but they denied him, so the punishment seized them while they were wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī doubles the outward messenger with an inner call: just as the Messenger came to them openly, signs reach them ceaselessly from within, through their own intimate thoughts; whoever does not answer these signs with concord and liberation, the punishment seizes him whence he does not expect. The denial of the Prophet redoubles that of the secret callings.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ع ذ ب

versets.ar: فَكُلُوا مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ اللَّهُ حَلَالًا طَيِّبًا وَاشْكُرُوا نِعْمَتَ اللَّهِ إِن كُنتُمْ إِيَّاهُ تَعْبُدُونَ
versets.tr: fa-kulū mimmā razaqakumu llāhu ḥalālan ṭayyiban wa-shkurū niʿmata llāhi in kuntum iyyāhu taʿbudūna
versets.trad: So eat of what God has provided you, lawful and good, and give thanks for the favor of God, if it is He whom you worship.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives a spiritual definition of the lawful and of gratitude: the lawful and good is what the servant takes under the condition of permission, with the witness of remembrance, by the courtesy that leaves the doubtful; and the reality of gratitude for the blessing is absence from the vision of the blessing through engulfment in the contemplation of the Benefactor. To give thanks is to forget the gift in the presence of the Giver.

mots.racine: ح ل ل / ط ي ب
ش ك ر / ن ع م

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا حَرَّمَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَيْتَةَ وَالدَّمَ وَلَحْمَ الْخِنزِيرِ وَمَا أُهِلَّ لِغَيْرِ اللَّهِ بِهِ ۖ فَمَنِ اضْطُرَّ غَيْرَ بَاغٍ وَلَا عَادٍ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: innamā ḥarrama ʿalaykumu l-maytata wa-d-dama wa-laḥma l-khinzīri wa-mā uhilla li-ghayri llāhi bihi fa-mani ḍṭurra ghayra bāghin wa-lā ʿādin fa-inna llāha ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: He has only forbidden you carrion, blood, the flesh of swine, and what has been dedicated to other than God. But whoever is compelled, neither rebellious nor transgressing — indeed God is forgiving, merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from the permission under necessity an allegory of the return to sobriety: just as the forbidden is permitted only under compulsion and in the strict measure that saves life, so the servant carried off by the overwhelmings of the Reality must return to the state of lucidity just enough to perform the prescribed duty — and he is not permitted to linger in the homelands of dispersion once the times of sobriety have passed for the sake of fulfilling the Law.

mots.racine: م و ت / د م و
ض ر ر / ب غ ي
mots.def: iḍṭurra: to be forced; bāghī: rebellious.

versets.ar: وَلَا تَقُولُوا لِمَا تَصِفُ أَلْسِنَتُكُمُ الْكَذِبَ هَٰذَا حَلَالٌ وَهَٰذَا حَرَامٌ لِّتَفْتَرُوا عَلَى اللَّهِ الْكَذِبَ ۚ إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى اللَّهِ الْكَذِبَ لَا يُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā taqūlū li-mā taṣifu alsinatukumu l-kadhiba hādhā ḥalālun wa-hādhā ḥarāmun li-taftarū ʿalā llāhi l-kadhiba inna lladhīna yaftarūna ʿalā llāhi l-kadhiba lā yufliḥūna
versets.trad: Do not say, as your tongues falsely describe, “This is lawful and this is forbidden,” so as to forge a lie against God. Those who forge lies against God will not prosper,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī praises truthfulness: sincerity in everything is better than lying; much of their objecting speech is but samples of falsehood. The veracious one (ṣiddīq) does not lie openly nor circulate the words of a base liar; upon the liar appears abasement for the slip he is in, and in the hereafter he will have a painful punishment.

mots.racine: و ص ف / ل س ن / ك ذ ب
ح ل ل / ح ر م

versets.ar: مَتَاعٌ قَلِيلٌ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: matāʿun qalīlun wa-lahum ʿadhābun alīmun
versets.trad: a brief enjoyment, and theirs is a painful punishment.
versets.commentaire: The verse continues the foregoing: the fleeting enjoyment of the lie is followed by torment. Qushayrī, in the thread of his commentary, opposes the brief delight of the liar to the duration of his penalty — a meager enjoyment here, and there a painful punishment — inviting one to prefer the truthfulness that lasts to the illusion that passes.

mots.racine: م ت ع / ق ل ل
ع ذ ب / أ ل م

versets.ar: وَعَلَى الَّذِينَ هَادُوا حَرَّمْنَا مَا قَصَصْنَا عَلَيْكَ مِن قَبْلُ ۖ وَمَا ظَلَمْنَاهُمْ وَلَٰكِن كَانُوا أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ʿalā lladhīna hādū ḥarramnā mā qaṣaṣnā ʿalayka min qablu wa-mā ẓalamnāhum wa-lākin kānū anfusahum yaẓlimūna
versets.trad: To those who follow Judaism We forbade what We related to you before. We did not wrong them, but they wronged themselves.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He showed that He had clearly set forth to the ancients the lawful and the forbidden; some did what they were commanded, others transgressed — and each was dealt with as he deserved: whoever obeyed, He drew his heart near; whoever disobeyed, He repelled and veiled him. The wrong comes not from God but from the choice of the soul.

mots.racine: ن ف س / ظ ل م

versets.ar: ثُمَّ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لِلَّذِينَ عَمِلُوا السُّوءَ بِجَهَالَةٍ ثُمَّ تَابُوا مِن بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ وَأَصْلَحُوا إِنَّ رَبَّكَ مِن بَعْدِهَا لَغَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: thumma inna rabbaka li-lladhīna ʿamilū s-sūʾa bi-jahālatin thumma tābū min baʿdi dhālika wa-aṣlaḥū inna rabbaka min baʿdihā la-ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: Yet know that toward those who did evil in ignorance, then repented after that and made amends, your Lord, after that, is forgiving, merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī paints the heart's conversion: when they repented of the ugliness of what they had done, grieved over all they had squandered and exceeded in, and the sincerity of their tears effaced the traces of their stumble — God looked upon them with mercy, accepted their return when they amended, and saved them when they implored.

mots.racine: ع م ل / س و أ / ج ه ل

versets.ar: إِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ كَانَ أُمَّةً قَانِتًا لِّلَّهِ حَنِيفًا وَلَمْ يَكُ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
versets.tr: inna ibrāhīma kāna ummatan qānitan li-llāhi ḥanīfan wa-lam yaku mina l-mushrikīna
versets.trad: Indeed Abraham was a nation, devoutly obedient to God, a pure monotheist, and he was not among the associators,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī probes why Abraham was “a nation” in himself: when he said of each thing he saw, “This is my Lord,” he did not regard the creatures as such but remained engulfed in the contemplation of the Real, seeing all the cosmos by God, mentioning nothing but God. So the Real rewarded him: you are the one who stands in the place of all, for in fulfilling God's right unceasingly there is sufficiency for the whole. The ḥanīf is the upright, wholly inclined toward the Real.

mots.def: umma: exemplar, a community in one man.

versets.ar: شَاكِرًا لِّأَنْعُمِهِ ۚ اجْتَبَاهُ وَهَدَاهُ إِلَىٰ صِرَاطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ
versets.tr: shākiran li-anʿumihi jtabāhu wa-hadāhu ilā ṣirāṭin mustaqīmin
versets.trad: grateful for His favors. He chose him and guided him to a straight path.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives the highest definition of the grateful one: the truly grateful is he who sees his own incapacity to give thanks, and who sees that his gratitude comes from God — knowing with certainty that it is He who created him, He who enabled him to thank, He who bestowed gratitude upon him, He who chose him until he was wholly His. The election (ijtibāʾ) crowns this self-stripping.

mots.racine: ش ك ر / ن ع م
ه د ي / ص ر ط
mots.sens: guided him to a straight path

versets.ar: وَآتَيْنَاهُ فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً ۖ وَإِنَّهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ لَمِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ
versets.tr: wa-ātaynāhu fī d-dunyā ḥasanatan wa-innahu fī l-ākhirati la-mina ṣ-ṣāliḥīna
versets.trad: We gave him good in this world, and in the hereafter he will surely be among the righteous.
themes: the fair portion of this world
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī interprets the “fair portion” (ḥasana) given to Abraham: it is the abiding of what He granted him, never cut off from him; and according to others, it is the intimate friendship (khulla), or prophethood. We gave him in this world a fair portion until he was wholly Ours, with no remainder left in him for another.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ح س ن

versets.ar: ثُمَّ أَوْحَيْنَا إِلَيْكَ أَنِ اتَّبِعْ مِلَّةَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ حَنِيفًا ۖ وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ
versets.tr: thumma awḥaynā ilayka ani ttabiʿ millata ibrāhīma ḥanīfan wa-mā kāna mina l-mushrikīna
versets.trad: Then We revealed to you: “Follow the religion of Abraham, a pure monotheist, who was not among the associators.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads the “religion of Abraham” (millat Ibrāhīm) as a spiritual state: being by the Real, and effacement from the witnessing of self. The Prophet, in following Abraham, obeyed God's command. And he adds: the way of Abraham was nobility, generosity, preferring others, and faithfulness; the Messenger followed it and surpassed in it, raising his rank above all.

mots.racine: ت ب ع / م ل ل
ك و ن / ش ر ك

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا جُعِلَ السَّبْتُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ اخْتَلَفُوا فِيهِ ۚ وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَيَحْكُمُ بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ فِيمَا كَانُوا فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ
versets.tr: innamā juʿila s-sabtu ʿalā lladhīna khtalafū fīhi wa-inna rabbaka la-yaḥkumu baynahum yawma l-qiyāmati fī-mā kānū fīhi yakhtalifūna
versets.trad: The Sabbath was made only for those who differed about it. Your Lord will surely judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that wherein they differed.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī recalls the historical dispute — some forbade work on it, others declared it lawful in disobedience; the Friday was assigned to them, but they said: we want only the Saturday — then gives the allusion: they deviated from what the command required and inclined to the side of their passion; having failed to observe it rightly, the Sabbath became the cause of their disobedience.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / س ب ت

versets.ar: ادْعُ إِلَىٰ سَبِيلِ رَبِّكَ بِالْحِكْمَةِ وَالْمَوْعِظَةِ الْحَسَنَةِ ۖ وَجَادِلْهُم بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَن ضَلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِ ۖ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِالْمُهْتَدِينَ
versets.tr: udʿu ilā sabīli rabbika bi-l-ḥikmati wa-l-mawʿiẓati l-ḥasanati wa-jādilhum bi-llatī hiya aḥsanu inna rabbaka huwa aʿlamu bi-man ḍalla ʿan sabīlihi wa-huwa aʿlamu bi-l-muhtadīna
versets.trad: Call to the way of your Lord with wisdom and beautiful exhortation, and argue with them in the best manner. Your Lord knows best who has strayed from His way, and He knows best the rightly guided.
versets.commentaire: The seal-verse of preaching. Qushayrī: to call to the way of God is to urge people to obey God and turn them from transgressing His command. The call by wisdom is not to contradict by act what one preaches by word; the beautiful exhortation is what proceeds from sound knowledge, without harshness; and arguing in the best manner is by the strongest proof and the clearest path. The condition of enjoining good: to do oneself what one commands and abstain from what one forbids.

mots.racine: د ع و / س ب ل
و ع ظ / ح س ن

versets.ar: وَإِنْ عَاقَبْتُمْ فَعَاقِبُوا بِمِثْلِ مَا عُوقِبْتُم بِهِ ۖ وَلَئِن صَبَرْتُمْ لَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لِّلصَّابِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-in ʿāqabtum fa-ʿāqibū bi-mithli mā ʿūqibtum bihi wa-la-in ṣabartum la-huwa khayrun li-ṣ-ṣābirīna
versets.trad: If you punish, then punish with the like of what was inflicted on you; but if you are patient, that is truly better for those who are patient.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī lists the motives for renouncing vengeance, in a rising gradation: one forgoes retaliation out of desire for the ampler reward of tomorrow; another so that God may take charge of his adversaries; another because he is content with God's knowledge of what he suffers; another out of nobility of soul and love of pardon in victory; and another, finally, recognizes for himself no right at all, his goods abandoned and his blood unclaimed. Patience is the higher portion.

mots.racine: م ث ل / ع ق ب
mots.sens: with the like of what was inflicted

versets.ar: وَاصْبِرْ وَمَا صَبْرُكَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ ۚ وَلَا تَحْزَنْ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا تَكُ فِي ضَيْقٍ مِّمَّا يَمْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ṣbir wa-mā ṣabruka illā bi-llāhi wa-lā taḥzan ʿalayhim wa-lā taku fī ḍayqin mimmā yamkurūna
versets.trad: Be patient — and your patience is only through God. Do not grieve over them, and do not be in distress over what they plot.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds one of his finest glosses: “Be patient” is a charge; “your patience is only through God” is a disclosure. “Be patient” realizes servanthood; “your patience is only through God” proclaims Lordship. And he consoles: look upon the decree: what We accord no weight should leave no trace in you; and since you know that We alone give existence, let not your heart be straitened by the violence of their enmity, for We have guaranteed to suffice you, not to let them gloat over you, and to open them no path to you.

mots.sens: your patience is only through God

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ مَعَ الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوا وَّالَّذِينَ هُم مُّحْسِنُونَ
versets.tr: inna llāha maʿa lladhīna ttaqaw wa-lladhīna hum muḥsinūna
versets.trad: Indeed God is with those who fear Him and those who do good.
versets.commentaire: Seal of the sūra. Qushayrī: God is with them by His support, and He encompasses them with beneficence and amplitude. “Those who fear Him” are those who guard against seeing the help come from any but Him, and who disavow all power and strength. And the beneficent (muḥsin) is he who worships God as if he saw Him — and that is the state of contemplation. The sūra of blessings ends upon the highest of graces: the divine companionship.

mots.def: muḥsin: one who acts with excellence.
